  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A join entity linking an issue to a release for release tracking. Each record represents an association between a single issue and a single release, along with metadata about the source of the link (e.g., which pull requests connected the issue to the release). Creating or deleting these associations automatically records the change in issue history.' on type 'IssueToRelease' has changed to 'A join entity linking an issue to a release for release tracking. Each record represents an association between a single issue and a single release, along with metadata about the source of the link (e.g., which pull requests connected the issue to the release). Creating or deleting these associations automatically records the change in issue history.' (IssueToRelease)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[ALPHA] Input for creating a new association between an issue and a release. Both an issue identifier and a release identifier must be provided.' on type 'IssueToReleaseCreateInput' has changed to 'Input for creating a new association between an issue and a release. Both an issue identifier and a release identifier must be provided.' (IssueToReleaseCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[ALPHA] The result of an issue-to-release mutation, containing the created or updated association and a success indicator.' on type 'IssueToReleasePayload' has changed to 'The result of an issue-to-release mutation, containing the created or updated association and a success indicator.' (IssueToReleasePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.issueToReleaseCreate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Creates a new association between an issue and a release, linking the issue to the release for tracking purposes.' to 'Creates a new association between an issue and a release, linking the issue to the release for tracking purposes.' (Mutation.issueToReleaseCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.issueToReleaseDelete' description changed from '[ALPHA] Deletes an issue-to-release association by its identifier, removing the issue from the release.' to 'Deletes an issue-to-release association by its identifier, removing the issue from the release.' (Mutation.issueToReleaseDelete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.issueToReleaseDeleteByIssueAndRelease' description changed from '[ALPHA] Deletes an issue-to-release association by looking up the issue and release identifiers, removing the issue from the release.' to 'Deletes an issue-to-release association by looking up the issue and release identifiers, removing the issue from the release.' (Mutation.issueToReleaseDeleteByIssueAndRelease)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseArchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Archives a release.' to 'Archives a release.' (Mutation.releaseArchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseComplete' description changed from '[ALPHA] Marks a release as completed. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release.' to 'Marks a release as completed. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release.' (Mutation.releaseComplete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseCompleteByAccessKey' description changed from '[ALPHA] Marks a release as completed using an access key. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release. The pipeline is inferred from the access key.' to 'Marks a release as completed using an access key. If version is provided, completes that specific release; otherwise completes the most recent started release. The pipeline is inferred from the access key.' (Mutation.releaseCompleteByAccessKey)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseCreate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Creates a new release in a pipeline. If no stage is specified, defaults to the first completed stage for continuous pipelines or the first started stage for scheduled pipelines.' to 'Creates a new release in a pipeline. If no stage is specified, defaults to the first completed stage for continuous pipelines or the first started stage for scheduled pipelines.' (Mutation.releaseCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseDelete' description changed from '[ALPHA] Moves a release to the trash bin. Trashed releases are archived and will be permanently deleted after a retention period. If the release is already archived, it is marked as trashed with a fresh archive timestamp.' to 'Moves a release to the trash bin. Trashed releases are archived and will be permanently deleted after a retention period. If the release is already archived, it is marked as trashed with a fresh archive timestamp.' (Mutation.releaseDelete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseNoteCreate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Creates a release note.' to 'Creates a release note.' (Mutation.releaseNoteCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseNoteDelete' description changed from '[ALPHA] Deletes a release note.' to 'Deletes a release note.' (Mutation.releaseNoteDelete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releasePipelineArchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Archives a release pipeline.' to 'Archives a release pipeline.' (Mutation.releasePipelineArchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releasePipelineCreate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Creates a new release pipeline with default stages. Subject to plan entitlement and quota limits.' to 'Creates a new release pipeline with default stages. Subject to plan entitlement and quota limits.' (Mutation.releasePipelineCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releasePipelineDelete' description changed from '[ALPHA] Permanently deletes a release pipeline and all associated stages and releases.' to 'Permanently deletes a release pipeline and all associated stages and releases.' (Mutation.releasePipelineDelete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releasePipelineUnarchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Unarchives a release pipeline.' to 'Unarchives a release pipeline.' (Mutation.releasePipelineUnarchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releasePipelineUpdate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Updates an existing release pipeline. Supports updating name, slug, type, production flag, path patterns, and team associations. Private teams that the current user cannot access are preserved in the team list.' to 'Updates an existing release pipeline. Supports updating name, slug, type, production flag, path patterns, and team associations. Private teams that the current user cannot access are preserved in the team list.' (Mutation.releasePipelineUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseStageArchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Archives a release stage. Only started-type stages can be archived, and only if they have no active releases and at least one other stage of the same type remains. Cannot archive the last non-frozen started stage.' to 'Archives a release stage. Only started-type stages can be archived, and only if they have no active releases and at least one other stage of the same type remains. Cannot archive the last non-frozen started stage.' (Mutation.releaseStageArchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseStageCreate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Creates a new release stage in a pipeline. Non-started stages must use default names and colors, and only one stage of each non-started type is allowed per pipeline. Started stages can optionally be frozen, but at least one non-frozen started stage must remain.' to 'Creates a new release stage in a pipeline. Non-started stages must use default names and colors, and only one stage of each non-started type is allowed per pipeline. Started stages can optionally be frozen, but at least one non-frozen started stage must remain.' (Mutation.releaseStageCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseStageUnarchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Unarchives a release stage.' to 'Unarchives a release stage.' (Mutation.releaseStageUnarchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseStageUpdate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Updates an existing release stage. Only started-type stages can be edited. Supports updating name, color, position, and frozen status.' to 'Updates an existing release stage. Only started-type stages can be edited. Supports updating name, color, position, and frozen status.' (Mutation.releaseStageUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseSync' description changed from '[ALPHA] Syncs release data by resolving issue and pull request references and associating them with a release. For continuous pipelines, creates a new completed release. For scheduled pipelines, finds or creates a started release and accumulates issues into it.' to 'Syncs release data by resolving issue and pull request references and associating them with a release. For continuous pipelines, creates a new completed release. For scheduled pipelines, finds or creates a started release and accumulates issues into it.' (Mutation.releaseSync)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseSyncByAccessKey' description changed from '[ALPHA] Syncs release data using an access key for CI/CD integration. The pipeline is automatically inferred from the access key's configured resources, so no pipeline ID is needed in the input.' to 'Syncs release data using an access key for CI/CD integration. The pipeline is automatically inferred from the access key's configured resources, so no pipeline ID is needed in the input.' (Mutation.releaseSyncByAccessKey)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseUnarchive' description changed from '[ALPHA] Unarchives a release.' to 'Unarchives a release.' (Mutation.releaseUnarchive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseUpdate' description changed from '[ALPHA] Updates an existing release by ID. Supports updating name, description, version, commit SHA, pipeline, stage, and dates.' to 'Updates an existing release by ID. Supports updating name, description, version, commit SHA, pipeline, stage, and dates.' (Mutation.releaseUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseUpdateByPipeline' description changed from '[ALPHA] Updates a release by pipeline identifier.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.releaseUpdateByPipelineByAccessKey' description changed from '[ALPHA] Updates a release by pipeline using an access key.' to 'Updates a release by pipeline using an access key.' (Mutation.releaseUpdateByPipelineByAccessKey)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release that bundles issues together for a software deployment or version. Releases belong to a release pipeline and progress through stages (e.g., planned, started, completed, canceled). Issues are associated with releases via the IssueToRelease join entity, and the release tracks lifecycle timestamps such as when it was started, completed, or canceled.' on type 'Release' has changed to 'A release that bundles issues together for a software deployment or version. Releases belong to a release pipeline and progress through stages (e.g., planned, started, completed, canceled). Issues are associated with releases via the IssueToRelease join entity, and the release tracks lifecycle timestamps such as when it was started, completed, or canceled.' (Release)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release history record containing a batch of chronologically ordered change events for a release. Each record holds up to 30 entries, and new records are created once the current record is full and a time window has elapsed. Tracks changes to name, description, version, stage, dates, pipeline, and archive status.' on type 'ReleaseHistory' has changed to 'A release history record containing a batch of chronologically ordered change events for a release. Each record holds up to 30 entries, and new records are created once the current record is full and a time window has elapsed. Tracks changes to name, description, version, stage, dates, pipeline, and archive status.' (ReleaseHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release pipeline that defines a release workflow with ordered stages. Pipelines can be continuous (each sync creates a completed release) or scheduled (issues accumulate in a started release that is explicitly completed). Pipelines are associated with teams and can filter commits by file path patterns.' on type 'ReleasePipeline' has changed to 'A release pipeline that defines a release workflow with ordered stages. Pipelines can be continuous (each sync creates a completed release) or scheduled (issues accumulate in a started release that is explicitly completed). Pipelines are associated with teams and can filter commits by file path patterns.' (ReleasePipeline)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A stage within a release pipeline that represents a phase in the release lifecycle (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Releases progress through stages as they move toward production. Started-type stages can be frozen to prevent new issues from being automatically synced into releases at that stage.' on type 'ReleaseStage' has changed to 'A stage within a release pipeline that represents a phase in the release lifecycle (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Releases progress through stages as they move toward production. Started-type stages can be frozen to prevent new issues from being automatically synced into releases at that stage.' (ReleaseStage)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ActorBot.avatarUrl' description changed from 'A url pointing to the avatar representing this bot.' to 'A URL pointing to the avatar image representing this bot, typically the integration's logo or icon.' (ActorBot.avatarUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ActorBot.id' has description 'A unique identifier for the bot actor.' (ActorBot.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ActorBot.subType' description changed from 'The sub type of the bot.' to 'A more specific classification within the bot type, providing additional context about the integration or application variant.' (ActorBot.subType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ActorBot.type' description changed from 'The type of bot.' to 'The source type of the bot, identifying the application or integration (e.g., 'github', 'slack', 'workflow', 'ai').' (ActorBot.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ActorBot.userDisplayName' description changed from 'The display name of the external user on behalf of which the bot acted.' to 'The display name of the external user on behalf of whom the bot acted. Shown when an integration action was triggered by a specific person in the external system.' (ActorBot.userDisplayName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A bot actor is an actor that is not a user, but an application or integration.' on type 'ActorBot' has changed to 'A bot actor representing a non-human entity that performed an action, such as an integration (GitHub, Slack, Zendesk), an AI assistant, or an automated workflow. Bot actors are displayed in activity feeds and history to indicate when changes were made by applications rather than users.' (ActorBot)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentActivity.content' description changed from 'The content of the activity' to 'The content of the activity, which varies by type (thought, action, response, prompt, error, or elicitation).' (AgentActivity.content)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentActivity.sourceComment' description changed from 'The comment this activity is linked to.' to 'The source comment this activity is linked to. Null if the activity was not triggered by a comment.' (AgentActivity.sourceComment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An activity within an agent context.' on type 'AgentActivity' has changed to 'An activity performed by or directed at an AI coding agent during a session. Activities represent the observable steps of an agent's work, including thoughts, actions (tool calls), responses, prompts from users, errors, and elicitation requests. Each activity belongs to an agent session and is associated with the user who initiated it.' (AgentActivity)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentActivityCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating an agent activity.' (AgentActivityCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentActivityPayload' has description 'The result of an agent activity mutation.' (AgentActivityPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'slugId' was added to object type 'AgentSession' (AgentSession.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.context' description changed from 'Serialized JSON representing the contexts this session is related to, for direct chat sessions.' to 'The entity contexts this session is related to, such as issues or projects referenced in direct chat sessions. Used to provide contextual awareness to the agent.' (AgentSession.context)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.dismissedAt' description changed from 'The time the agent session was dismissed.' to 'The time a user dismissed this agent session. When dismissed, the agent is removed as delegate from the associated issue. Null if the session has not been dismissed.' (AgentSession.dismissedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.dismissedBy' description changed from 'The user who dismissed the agent session.' to 'The user who dismissed the agent session. Automatically set when dismissedAt is updated. Null if the session has not been dismissed.' (AgentSession.dismissedBy)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.endedAt' description changed from 'The time the agent session ended.' to 'The time the agent session completed. Null if the session is still in progress or was dismissed before completion.' (AgentSession.endedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.plan' description changed from 'A dynamically updated list of the agent's execution strategy.' to 'A dynamically updated plan describing the agent's execution strategy, including steps to be taken and their current status. Updated as the agent progresses through its work. Null if no plan has been set.' (AgentSession.plan)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.startedAt' description changed from 'The time the agent session started.' to 'The time the agent session transitioned to active status and began work. Null if the session has not yet started.' (AgentSession.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.status' description changed from 'The current status of the agent session.' to 'The current status of the agent session, such as pending, active, awaiting input, complete, error, or stale.' (AgentSession.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.summary' description changed from 'A summary of the activities in this session.' to 'A human-readable summary of the work performed in this session. Null if no summary has been generated yet.' (AgentSession.summary)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AgentSession.url' description changed from 'Agent session URL.' to 'The URL to the agent session page in the Linear app. Null for direct chat sessions without an associated issue.' (AgentSession.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A session for agent activities and state management.' on type 'AgentSession' has changed to 'A session representing an AI coding agent's work on an issue or conversation. Agent sessions track the lifecycle of an agent's engagement, from creation through active work to completion or dismissal. Each session is associated with an agent user (the bot), optionally a human creator, an issue, and a comment thread where the agent posts updates. Sessions contain activities that record the agent's observable steps and can be linked to pull requests created during the work.' (AgentSession)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentSessionCreateInput' has description '[Internal] Input for creating an agent session on behalf of the current user.' (AgentSessionCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentSessionCreateOnComment' has description 'Input for creating an agent session on a root comment.' (AgentSessionCreateOnComment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentSessionCreateOnIssue' has description 'Input for creating an agent session on an issue.' (AgentSessionCreateOnIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Join table between agent sessions and pull requests.' on type 'AgentSessionToPullRequest' has changed to 'A link between an agent session and a pull request created or associated during that session. This join entity tracks which pull requests were produced by or connected to a coding agent's work session, and handles backfilling links when pull requests are synced after the agent has already recorded the URL.' (AgentSessionToPullRequest)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentSessionUpdateExternalUrlInput' has description 'Input for updating the external URLs of an agent session.' (AgentSessionUpdateExternalUrlInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'AgentSessionUpdateInput.dismissedAt' description changed from '[Internal] The time the agent session was dismissed. Only updatable by internal clients.' to '[Internal] The time at which the agent session was dismissed. Set to null to un-dismiss. Only updatable by internal clients.' (AgentSessionUpdateInput.dismissedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AgentSessionUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an agent session.' (AgentSessionUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'clientPlatform' was added to object type 'AiConversation' (AiConversation.clientPlatform)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'slugId' was added to object type 'AiConversation' (AiConversation.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.context' description changed from 'Serialized JSON representing the contexts this conversation is related to.' to 'The entity contexts this conversation is related to, such as issues, projects, or documents that provide context for the AI assistant.' (AiConversation.context)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.initialSource' description changed from 'The initial source of the conversation.' to 'The source from which this conversation was initiated, such as direct chat, an issue comment, a Slack thread, Microsoft Teams, or a workflow automation.' (AiConversation.initialSource)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.iterationId' description changed from 'The iteration ID of the conversation in agentic workflow.' to 'The iteration ID when this conversation is part of an agentic workflow. Used to track multi-step workflow executions. Null for non-workflow conversations.' (AiConversation.iterationId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.parts' description changed from 'The parts of the conversation.' to 'The ordered sequence of conversation parts (prompts, text responses, reasoning steps, tool calls, and widgets) that make up this conversation's visible history.' (AiConversation.parts)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.readAt' description changed from 'The time at when the user marked the conversation as read. Null, if the user hasn't read the conversation.' to 'The time when the user marked the conversation as read. Null if the user hasn't read the conversation.' (AiConversation.readAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversation.status' description changed from 'The status of the conversation.' to 'The current processing status of the conversation, indicating whether the AI is actively generating a response or has completed its turn.' (AiConversation.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A conversation between a user and an LLM.' on type 'AiConversation' has changed to '[Internal] A conversation between a user and the Linear AI assistant. Each conversation tracks the full thread state, context references (issues, projects, documents, etc.), and conversation parts (prompts, text responses, tool calls, widgets). Conversations can originate from direct chat, issue comments, Slack threads, Microsoft Teams, or automated workflows.' (AiConversation)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversationPartMetadata.endedAt' description changed from 'The ended timestamp of the part.' to 'The time when the part ended, as an ISO 8601 string.' (AiConversationPartMetadata.endedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AiConversationPartMetadata.startedAt' description changed from 'The started timestamp of the part.' to 'The time when the part started, as an ISO 8601 string.' (AiConversationPartMetadata.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A prompt workflow progress.' on type 'AiPromptProgress' has changed to '[Internal] Tracks the progress and state of an AI prompt workflow execution. Each progress record is associated with an issue or comment and contains the workflow type, current status, metadata about the execution, and optionally the conversation messages exchanged during the workflow. Progress records can form a hierarchy via parent-child relationships for multi-step workflows.' (AiPromptProgress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'AI prompt rules for a team.' on type 'AiPromptRules' has changed to 'Custom rules that guide AI behavior for a specific scope. Rules can be defined at the workspace level, team level, integration level, or user level, and are applied hierarchically (workspace rules first, then parent team rules, then team rules). Rules contain structured content that instructs the AI assistant on how to handle specific types of prompts, such as coding agent guidance or triage intelligence configuration.' (AiPromptRules)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Application.developerUrl' description changed from 'Url of the developer (homepage or docs).' to 'URL of the developer's website, homepage, or documentation.' (Application.developerUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Public information of the OAuth application.' on type 'Application' has changed to 'Public-facing information about an OAuth application. Contains only the fields that are safe to display to users during the authorization flow, excluding sensitive data like client secrets and internal configuration.' (Application)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Attachment.groupBySource' description changed from 'Indicates if attachments for the same source application should be grouped in the Linear UI.' to 'Whether attachments from the same source application should be visually grouped together in the Linear issue detail view.' (Attachment.groupBySource)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Attachment.metadata' description changed from 'Custom metadata related to the attachment.' to 'Integration-specific metadata for this attachment. The schema varies by source type and may include fields such as pull request status, review counts, commit information, ticket status, or other data from the external system.' (Attachment.metadata)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Attachment.originalIssue' description changed from 'The issue this attachment was originally created on. Will be undefined if the attachment hasn't been moved.' to 'The issue this attachment was originally created on. Null if the attachment hasn't been moved.' (Attachment.originalIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Attachment.sourceType' description changed from 'An accessor helper to source.type, defines the source type of the attachment.' to 'The source type of the attachment, derived from the source metadata. Returns the integration type (e.g., 'github', 'slack', 'zendesk') or 'unknown' if no source is set.' (Attachment.sourceType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Attachment.url' description changed from 'Location of the attachment which is also used as an identifier.' to 'The URL of the external resource this attachment links to. Also serves as a unique identifier for the attachment within an issue; no two attachments on the same issue can share the same URL.' (Attachment.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Issue attachment (e.g. support ticket, pull request).' on type 'Attachment' has changed to 'An attachment linking external content to an issue. Attachments represent connections to external resources such as GitHub pull requests, Slack messages, Zendesk tickets, Figma files, Sentry issues, Intercom conversations, and plain URLs. Each attachment has a title and subtitle displayed in the Linear UI, a URL serving as both the link destination and unique identifier per issue, and optional metadata specific to the source integration.' (Attachment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AttachmentCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new issue attachment.' (AttachmentCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AttachmentPayload' has description 'The result of an attachment mutation.' (AttachmentPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AttachmentSourcesPayload' has description 'The result of an attachment sources query.' (AttachmentSourcesPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'AttachmentUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing issue attachment.' (AttachmentUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AuditEntry.countryCode' description changed from 'Country code of request resulting to audit entry.' to 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code derived from the request IP address. Null if geo-location could not be determined.' (AuditEntry.countryCode)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AuditEntry.ip' description changed from 'IP from actor when entry was recorded.' to 'The IP address of the actor at the time the audited action was performed. Null if the IP was not captured.' (AuditEntry.ip)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AuditEntry.organization' description changed from 'The organization the audit log belongs to.' to 'The workspace the audit log belongs to.' (AuditEntry.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'AuditEntry.type' has description 'The type of audited action (e.g., user authentication, permission change, data export, setting modification).' (AuditEntry.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Workspace audit log entry object.' on type 'AuditEntry' has changed to 'A workspace audit log entry recording a security or compliance-relevant action. Audit entries capture who performed an action, when, from what IP address and country, and include type-specific metadata. The audit log is partitioned by time for performance and is accessible only to workspace administrators. Examples of audited actions include user authentication events, permission changes, data exports, and workspace setting modifications.' (AuditEntry)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Authentication session information.' on type 'AuthenticationSessionResponse' has changed to 'Information about an active authentication session, including the device, location, and timestamps for when it was created and last used.'   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.parent' description changed from 'The parent comment under which the current comment is nested.' to 'The parent comment under which the current comment is nested. Null for top-level comments that are not replies.' (Comment.parent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.parentId' description changed from 'The ID of the parent comment under which the current comment is nested.' to 'The ID of the parent comment under which the current comment is nested. Null for top-level comments.' (Comment.parentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.post' description changed from 'The post that the comment is associated with.' to 'The post that the comment is associated with. Null if the comment belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Comment.post)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.quotedText' description changed from 'The text that this comment references. Each entry contains the emoji name, count, and the IDs of users who reacted.' (Comment.reactionData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.resolvedAt' description changed from 'The time the resolvingUser resolved the thread.' to 'The time when the comment thread was resolved. Null if the thread is unresolved.' (Comment.resolvedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.resolvingComment' description changed from 'The comment that resolved the thread.' to 'The child comment that resolved this thread. Only set on top-level (parent) comments. Null if the thread is unresolved.' (Comment.resolvingComment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.resolvingCommentId' description changed from 'The ID of the comment that resolved the thread.' to 'The ID of the child comment that resolved this thread. Null if the thread is unresolved.' (Comment.resolvingCommentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.resolvingUser' description changed from 'The user that resolved the thread.' to 'The user that resolved the comment thread. Null if the thread has not been resolved or if this is not a top-level comment.' (Comment.resolvingUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.threadSummary' description changed from '[Internal] A generated summary of the comment thread.' to '[Internal] An AI-generated summary of the comment thread. Null if no summary has been generated or if this is not a top-level comment.' (Comment.threadSummary)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Comment.user' description changed from 'The user who wrote the comment.' to 'The user who wrote the comment. Null for comments created by integrations or bots without a user association.' (Comment.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A comment associated with an entity.' on type 'Comment' has changed to 'A comment associated with an issue, project update, initiative update, document content, post, project, or initiative. Comments support rich text (ProseMirror), emoji reactions, and threaded replies via parentId. Comments can be created by workspace users or by external users through integrations (e.g., Slack, Intercom). Each comment belongs to exactly one parent entity.' (Comment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CommentCreateInput.createdAt' description changed from 'The date when the comment was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a date in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.' to 'The time at which the comment was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.' (CommentCreateInput.createdAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactCreateInput.message' description changed from 'The message the user sent.' to 'The feedback or support message submitted by the user.' (ContactCreateInput.message)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ContactPayload' has description 'Return type for contact mutations.' (ContactPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.companySize' description changed from 'Size of the company.' to 'The size of the inquiring company (e.g., '1-19', '20-99', '100-499'). Used to route the inquiry to the appropriate sales channel.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.companySize)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.distinctId' description changed from 'PostHog distinct ID for analytics correlation.' to 'PostHog distinct ID for correlating this inquiry with anonymous analytics events.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.distinctId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.email' description changed from 'Work email of the person requesting information.' to 'Work email address of the person submitting the sales inquiry.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.email)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.message' description changed from 'The message the user sent.' to 'An optional message from the user describing their needs or questions.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.message)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.name' description changed from 'Name of the person requesting information.' to 'Full name of the person submitting the sales inquiry.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ContactSalesCreateInput.url' description changed from 'The URL this request was sent from.' to 'The page URL from which the sales inquiry was submitted, for attribution tracking.' (ContactSalesCreateInput.url)

Small companies are routed to Intercom support, while larger companies are routed to HubSpot.' (ContactSalesCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'CreateCsvExportReportPayload' has description 'The payload returned by the createCsvExportReport mutation.' (CreateCsvExportReportPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.creator' description changed from 'The user who created the custom view.' to 'The user who originally created the custom view.' (CustomView.creator)

Null if the view is not attached to any parent via a facet.' (CustomView.facet)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.feedItemFilterData' description changed from 'The filter applied to feed items in the custom view.' to 'The filter applied to feed items in the custom view. When set, this view displays feed items (updates) instead of issues.' (CustomView.feedItemFilterData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.filterData' description changed from 'The filter applied to issues in the custom view.' to 'The structured filter applied to issues in the custom view. Used when the view's modelName is "Issue".' (CustomView.filterData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.filters' description changed from 'The filters applied to issues in the custom view.' to 'The legacy serialized filters applied to issues in the custom view.' (CustomView.filters)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.icon' description changed from 'The icon of the custom view.' to 'The icon of the custom view. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon identifier.' (CustomView.icon)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.initiativeFilterData' description changed from 'The filter applied to initiatives in the custom view.' to 'The filter applied to initiatives in the custom view. When set, this view displays initiatives instead of issues.' (CustomView.initiativeFilterData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.initiatives' description changed from 'Initiatives associated with the custom view.' to 'Initiatives matching the custom view's initiative filter. Returns an empty connection if the view's modelName is not "Initiative".' (CustomView.initiatives)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.issues' description changed from 'Issues associated with the custom view.' to 'Issues matching the custom view's issue filter. Returns an empty connection if the view's modelName is not "Issue".' (CustomView.issues)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.modelName' description changed from 'The model name of the custom view.' to 'The entity type this view displays. Determined by which filter is set: "Project" if projectFilterData is set, "Initiative" if initiativeFilterData is set, "FeedItem" if feedItemFilterData is set, or "Issue" by default.' (CustomView.modelName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.name' description changed from 'The name of the custom view.' to 'The name of the custom view, displayed in the sidebar and navigation.' (CustomView.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the custom view.' to 'The workspace of the custom view.' (CustomView.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.organizationViewPreferences' description changed from 'The organizations default view preferences for this custom view.' to 'The workspace-level default view preferences for this custom view, if any have been set.' (CustomView.organizationViewPreferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.owner' description changed from 'The user who owns the custom view.' to 'The user who owns the custom view. For personal views, only the owner can see and edit the view.' (CustomView.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.projectFilterData' description changed from 'The filter applied to projects in the custom view.' to 'The filter applied to projects in the custom view. When set, this view displays projects instead of issues.' (CustomView.projectFilterData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.projects' description changed from 'Projects associated with the custom view.' to 'Projects matching the custom view's project filter. Returns an empty connection if the view's modelName is not "Project".' (CustomView.projects)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.shared' description changed from 'Whether the custom view is shared with everyone in the organization.' to 'Whether the custom view is shared with everyone in the organization. Shared views appear in the workspace sidebar for all members. Personal (non-shared) views are only visible to their owner.' (CustomView.shared)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.slugId' description changed from 'The custom view's unique URL slug.' to 'The custom view's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs. Automatically generated on creation.' (CustomView.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.team' description changed from 'The team associated with the custom view.' to 'The team that the custom view is scoped to. Null if the view is workspace-wide or scoped to a project/initiative instead.' (CustomView.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.updatedBy' description changed from 'The user who last updated the custom view.' to 'The user who last updated the custom view. Null if the updater's account has been deleted.' (CustomView.updatedBy)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.updates' description changed from 'Feed items associated with the custom view.' to 'Feed items (updates) matching the custom view's feed item filter. Returns an empty connection if the view's modelName is not "FeedItem".' (CustomView.updates)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.userViewPreferences' description changed from 'The current users view preferences for this custom view.' to 'The current user's personal view preferences for this custom view, if they have set any.' (CustomView.userViewPreferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomView.viewPreferencesValues' description changed from 'The calculated view preferences values for this custom view.' to 'The computed view preferences values for this custom view, merging organization defaults with user overrides and system defaults. Use this for the effective display settings rather than reading raw preferences directly.' (CustomView.viewPreferencesValues)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A custom view that has been saved by a user.' on type 'CustomView' has changed to 'A custom view built from a saved filter, sort, and grouping configuration. Views can be personal (visible only to the owner) or shared with the entire workspace. They define which issues, projects, initiatives, or feed items are displayed and how they are organized. Views can optionally be scoped to a team, project, or initiative.' (CustomView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomViewCreateInput.shared' description changed from 'Whether the custom view is shared with everyone in the organization.' to 'Whether the custom view is shared with everyone in the workspace.' (CustomViewCreateInput.shared)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomViewNotificationSubscription.contextViewType' description changed from 'The type of view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.approximateNeedCount' description changed from 'The approximate number of needs of the customer.' to 'The approximate count of customer needs (requests) associated with this customer.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.domains' description changed from 'The domains associated with this customer.' to 'The email domains associated with this customer (e.g., 'acme.com'). Used to automatically match incoming requests to this customer. Public email domains (e.g., gmail.com) are not allowed. Domains must be unique across all customers in the workspace.' (Customer.domains)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.externalIds' description changed from 'The ids of the customers in external systems.' to 'Identifiers for this customer in external systems (e.g., CRM IDs from Intercom, Salesforce, or HubSpot).   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.integration' description changed from 'The integration that manages the Customer.' to 'The integration that manages this customer's data (e.g., Intercom, Salesforce). Null if the customer is not managed by any data source integration.' (Customer.integration)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.logoUrl' description changed from 'The customer's logo URL.' to 'URL of the customer's logo image. Null if no logo has been uploaded.' (Customer.logoUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.mainSourceId' description changed from 'The ID of the main source, when a customer has multiple sources. Must be one of externalIds.' to 'The primary external source ID when a customer has data from multiple external systems. Must be one of the values in the externalIds array. Null if the customer has zero or one external source.' (Customer.mainSourceId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.name' description changed from 'The customer's name.' to 'The display name of the customer organization.' (Customer.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.needs' description changed from 'Customer needs associated with this customer.' to 'The list of customer needs (product requests and feedback) associated with this customer.' (Customer.needs)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.owner' description changed from 'The user who owns the customer.' to 'The workspace member assigned as the owner of this customer. Null if no owner has been assigned. App users cannot be set as customer owners.' (Customer.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.revenue' description changed from 'The annual revenue generated by the customer.' to 'The annual revenue generated by this customer. Null if revenue data has not been provided. May be synced from an external data source such as a CRM integration.' (Customer.revenue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.size' description changed from 'The size of the customer.' to 'The number of employees or seats at the customer organization. Null if size data has not been provided. May be synced from an external data source such as a CRM integration.' (Customer.size)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.slackChannelId' description changed from 'The ID of the Slack channel used to interact with the customer.' to 'The ID of the Slack channel linked to this customer for communication. Null if no Slack channel has been associated. Must be unique across all customers in the workspace.' (Customer.slackChannelId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.slugId' description changed from 'The customer's unique URL slug.' to 'A unique, human-readable URL slug for the customer. Automatically generated and used in customer page URLs.' (Customer.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.status' description changed from 'The current status of the customer.' to 'The current lifecycle status of the customer. Defaults to the first status by position when a customer is created without an explicit status.' (Customer.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.tier' description changed from 'The tier of the customer.' to 'The tier or segment assigned to this customer for prioritization (e.g., Enterprise, Pro, Free). Null if no tier has been assigned.' (Customer.tier)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Customer.url' description changed from 'URL of the customer in Linear.' to 'The URL of the customer's page in the Linear application.' (Customer.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A customer whose needs will be tied to issues or projects.' on type 'Customer' has changed to 'A customer organization tracked in Linear's customer management system. Customers represent external companies or organizations whose product requests and feedback are captured as customer needs, which can be linked to issues and projects. Customers can be associated with domains, external system IDs, Slack channels, and managed by integrations such as Intercom or Salesforce.' (Customer)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.domains' description changed from 'The domains associated with this customer.' to 'The email domains associated with this customer (e.g., 'acme.com'). Public email domains are not allowed. Defaults to an empty array.' (CustomerCreateInput.domains)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.logoUrl' description changed from 'The URL of the customer's logo.' to 'The URL of the customer's logo image.' (CustomerCreateInput.logoUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.mainSourceId' description changed from 'The main source of the customer, for customers with multiple sources.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.name' description changed from 'The name of the customer.' to 'The display name of the customer organization.' (CustomerCreateInput.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.ownerId' description changed from 'The user who owns the customer.' to 'The identifier of the user to assign as the owner of the customer.' (CustomerCreateInput.ownerId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.revenue' description changed from 'The annual revenue generated by the customer.' to 'The annual revenue generated by the customer, in dollars.' (CustomerCreateInput.revenue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.size' description changed from 'The size of the customer.' to 'The size of the customer organization (e.g., number of employees).' (CustomerCreateInput.size)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.statusId' description changed from 'The status of the customer.' to 'The identifier of the customer status to set.' (CustomerCreateInput.statusId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerCreateInput.tierId' description changed from 'The tier of the customer customer.' to 'The identifier of the customer tier to assign.' (CustomerCreateInput.tierId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'CustomerCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new customer in the workspace.' (CustomerCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.attachment' description changed from 'The attachment this need is referencing.' to 'The issue attachment linked to this need. Populated when the need originates from an intake source (e.g., Slack, Intercom) or when a URL is manually provided. Provides a link back to the original source of the customer feedback. Mutually exclusive with projectAttachment.' (CustomerNeed.attachment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.body' description changed from 'The need content in markdown format.' to 'The body content of the need in Markdown format. Used to capture manual input about needs that cannot be directly tied to an attachment. Null if the need's content comes from an attached source.' (CustomerNeed.body)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.bodyData' description changed from '[Internal] The content of the need as a Prosemirror document.' to '[Internal] The body content of the need as a Prosemirror document JSON string. This is the structured representation of the body field, used for rich text rendering in the editor.' (CustomerNeed.bodyData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.comment' description changed from 'The comment this need is referencing.' to 'An optional comment providing additional context for this need. Null if the need was not created from or associated with a specific comment.' (CustomerNeed.comment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.creator' description changed from 'The creator of the customer need.' to 'The user who manually created this customer need. Null for needs created automatically by integrations or intake sources.' (CustomerNeed.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.customer' description changed from 'The customer that this need is attached to.' to 'The customer organization this need belongs to. Null if the need has not yet been associated with a customer.' (CustomerNeed.customer)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.issue' description changed from 'The issue this need is referencing.' to 'The issue this need is linked to. Either issueId or projectId must be set. When set, the need's projectId is denormalized from the issue's project.' (CustomerNeed.issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.originalIssue' description changed from 'The issue this customer need was originally created on. Will be undefined if the customer need hasn't been moved.' to 'The issue this customer need was originally created on, before being moved to a different issue or project. Null if the customer need has not been moved from its original location.' (CustomerNeed.originalIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.project' description changed from 'The project this need is referencing.' to 'The project this need is linked to. For issue-based needs, this is denormalized from the issue's project. For project-only needs, this is set directly.' (CustomerNeed.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.projectAttachment' description changed from 'The project attachment this need is referencing.' to 'The project attachment linked to this need. Populated when the need originates from an intake source or when a URL is manually provided for a project-level need. Provides a link back to the original source of the customer feedback. Mutually exclusive with attachment.' (CustomerNeed.projectAttachment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CustomerNeed.url' description changed from 'The URL of the underlying attachment, if any' to 'The URL of the source attachment linked to this need, if any. Returns the URL from either the issue attachment or project attachment. Null if the need has no attached source.' (CustomerNeed.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A customer need, expressed through a reference to an issue, project, or comment.' on type 'CustomerNeed' has changed to 'A customer need represents a specific product request or piece of feedback from a customer. Needs can be created manually, from integrations, or from intake sources like email.' (CustomerNeed)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerNeedCreateFromAttachmentInput.attachmentId' description changed from 'The attachment this need is created from.' to 'The UUID of the existing issue attachment to create a customer need from.' (CustomerNeedCreateFromAttachmentInput.attachmentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CustomerNeedCreateInput.body' description changed from 'The content of the need in markdown format.' to 'The body content of the need in Markdown format. Cannot be used together with bodyData.' (CustomerNeedCreateInput.body)

(CustomerUpsertInput.tierName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'CustomerUpsertInput' has description 'Input for upserting a customer. Matches against existing customers using id, externalId, slackChannelId, or domains. Creates a new customer if no match is found.' (CustomerUpsertInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.autoArchivedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the cycle was automatically archived by the auto pruning process.' to 'The time at which the cycle was automatically archived by the auto-pruning process. Null if the cycle has not been auto-archived.' (Cycle.autoArchivedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.completedAt' description changed from 'The completion time of the cycle. If null, the cycle hasn't been completed.' to 'The completion time of the cycle. If null, the cycle has not been completed yet. A cycle is completed either when its end date passes or when it is manually completed early.' (Cycle.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.completedIssueCountHistory' description changed from 'The number of completed issues in the cycle after each day.' to 'The number of completed issues in the cycle after each day. Each entry corresponds to the same day index as issueCountHistory.' (Cycle.completedIssueCountHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.completedScopeHistory' description changed from 'The number of completed estimation points after each day.' to 'The number of completed estimation points after each day. Used together with scopeHistory for burndown charts.' (Cycle.completedScopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.currentProgress' description changed from '[Internal] The current progress of the cycle.' to '[Internal] The current progress snapshot of the cycle, broken down by issue status categories.' (Cycle.currentProgress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.description' description changed from 'The cycle's description.' to 'The description of the cycle.' (Cycle.description)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.endsAt' description changed from 'The end time of the cycle.' to 'The end date and time of the cycle. When a cycle is completed prematurely, this is updated to match the completion time. When cycles are disabled, both endsAt and completedAt are set to the current time.' (Cycle.endsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.inProgressScopeHistory' description changed from 'The number of in progress estimation points after each day.' to 'The number of in-progress estimation points after each day. Tracks work that has been started but not yet completed.' (Cycle.inProgressScopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.inheritedFrom' description changed from 'The cycle inherited from.' to 'The parent cycle this cycle was inherited from. When a parent team creates cycles, sub-teams automatically receive corresponding inherited cycles.' (Cycle.inheritedFrom)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.isActive' description changed from 'Whether the cycle is currently active.' to 'Whether the cycle is currently active. A cycle is active if the current time is between its start and end dates and it has not been completed.' (Cycle.isActive)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.isFuture' description changed from 'Whether the cycle is in the future.' to 'Whether the cycle has not yet started. True if the cycle's start date is in the future.' (Cycle.isFuture)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.isNext' description changed from 'Whether the cycle is the next cycle for the team.' to 'Whether this cycle is the next upcoming (not yet started) cycle for the team.' (Cycle.isNext)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.isPast' description changed from 'Whether the cycle is in the past.' to 'Whether the cycle's end date has passed.' (Cycle.isPast)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.isPrevious' description changed from 'Whether the cycle is the previous cycle for the team.' to 'Whether this cycle is the most recently completed cycle for the team.' (Cycle.isPrevious)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.issueCountHistory' description changed from 'The total number of issues in the cycle after each day.' to 'The total number of issues in the cycle after each day. Each entry represents a snapshot at the end of that day, forming the basis for burndown charts.' (Cycle.issueCountHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.issues' description changed from 'Issues associated with the cycle.' to 'Issues that are currently assigned to this cycle.' (Cycle.issues)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.name' description changed from 'The custom name of the cycle.' to 'The custom name of the cycle. If not set, the cycle is displayed using its number (e.g., "Cycle 5").' (Cycle.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.number' description changed from 'The number of the cycle.' to 'The auto-incrementing number of the cycle, unique within its team. This value is assigned automatically by the database and cannot be set on creation.' (Cycle.number)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.progress' description changed from 'The overall progress of the cycle. This is the (completed estimate points + 0.25 _ in progress estimate points) / total estimate points.' to 'The overall progress of the cycle as a number between 0 and 1. Calculated as (completed estimate points + 0.25 _ in-progress estimate points) / total estimate points. Returns 0 if no estimate points exist.' (Cycle.progress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.progressHistory' description changed from '[Internal] The progress history of the cycle.' to '[Internal] The detailed progress history of the cycle, including per-status breakdowns over time.' (Cycle.progressHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.scopeHistory' description changed from 'The total number of estimation points after each day.' to 'The total number of estimation points (scope) in the cycle after each day. Used for scope-based burndown charts.' (Cycle.scopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.startsAt' description changed from 'The start time of the cycle.' to 'The start date and time of the cycle.' (Cycle.startsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.team' description changed from 'The team that the cycle is associated with.' to 'The team that the cycle belongs to. Each cycle is scoped to exactly one team.' (Cycle.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Cycle.uncompletedIssuesUponClose' description changed from 'Issues that weren't completed when the cycle was closed.' to 'Issues that were still open (not completed) when the cycle was closed. These issues may have been moved to the next cycle.' (Cycle.uncompletedIssuesUponClose)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A set of issues to be resolved in a specified amount of time.' on type 'Cycle' has changed to 'A time-boxed iteration (similar to a sprint) used for planning and tracking work. Cycles belong to a team and have defined start and end dates. Issues are assigned to cycles for time-based planning, and progress is tracked via completed, in-progress, and total scope. Cycles are automatically completed when their end date passes, and uncompleted issues can be carried over to the next cycle.' (Cycle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CycleCreateInput.endsAt' description changed from 'The end date of the cycle.' to 'The end time of the cycle.' (CycleCreateInput.endsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CycleCreateInput.startsAt' description changed from 'The start date of the cycle.' to 'The start time of the cycle.' (CycleCreateInput.startsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'CycleCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new cycle.' (CycleCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.active' description changed from 'Whether the subscription is active or not.' to 'Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.active)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.contextViewType' description changed from 'The type of view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.initiative' description changed from 'The contextual initiative view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The initiative that this notification subscription is scoped to.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.label' description changed from 'The contextual label view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes' description changed from 'The type of subscription.' to 'The notification event types that this subscription will deliver to the subscriber.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.project' description changed from 'The contextual project view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The project that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.subscriber' description changed from 'The user that subscribed to receive notifications.' to 'The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.subscriber)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.team' description changed from 'The team associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The team that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.user' description changed from 'The user view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The user that this notification subscription is scoped to, for user-specific view subscriptions. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'CycleNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType' description changed from 'The type of user view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.' (CycleNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A cycle notification subscription.' on type 'CycleNotificationSubscription' has changed to 'A notification subscription scoped to a specific cycle.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Input for shifting all cycles from a certain cycle onwards by a certain number of days' on type 'CycleShiftAllInput' has changed to 'Input for shifting all cycles from a certain cycle onwards by a certain number of days.' (CycleShiftAllInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CycleUpdateInput.completedAt' description changed from 'The end date of the cycle.' to 'The completion time of the cycle. If null, the cycle hasn't been completed.' (CycleUpdateInput.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'CycleUpdateInput.endsAt' description changed from 'The end date of the cycle.' to 'The end time of the cycle.' (CycleUpdateInput.endsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.color' description changed from 'The color of the icon of the dashboard.' to 'The hex color code of the dashboard icon.' (Dashboard.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.creator' description changed from 'The user who created the dashboard.' to 'The user who created the dashboard. Null if the creator's account has been deleted.' (Dashboard.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.icon' description changed from 'The icon of the dashboard.' to 'The icon of the dashboard. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon identifier.' (Dashboard.icon)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.issueFilter' description changed from 'The filter applied to all dashboard widgets showing issues data.' to 'A global issue filter applied to all dashboard widgets that display issue data. Individual widgets may apply additional filters on top of this.' (Dashboard.issueFilter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the dashboard.' to 'The organization that the dashboard belongs to.' (Dashboard.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.owner' description changed from 'The owner of the dashboard.' to 'The owner of the dashboard. For personal dashboards, only the owner can view them. Null if the owner's account has been deleted.' (Dashboard.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.projectFilter' description changed from 'The filter applied to all dashboard widgets showing projects data.' to 'A global project filter applied to all dashboard widgets that display project data. Individual widgets may apply additional filters on top of this.' (Dashboard.projectFilter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.shared' description changed from 'Whether the dashboard is shared with everyone in the organization.' to 'Whether the dashboard is shared with everyone in the workspace. Shared dashboards are visible to all members; unshared dashboards are only visible to the owner.' (Dashboard.shared)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.slugId' description changed from 'The dashboard's unique URL slug.' to 'The dashboard's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs. Automatically generated on creation.' (Dashboard.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the dashboard within the organization or its team.' to 'The sort order of the dashboard within the workspace or its team. Lower values appear first.' (Dashboard.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Dashboard.updatedBy' description changed from 'The user who last updated the dashboard.' to 'The user who last updated the dashboard. Null if no user has updated it or the updater's account has been deleted.' (Dashboard.updatedBy)

Null if the content belongs to a different parent entity type.' (DocumentContent.welcomeMessage)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A document content for a project.' on type 'DocumentContent' has changed to 'The rich-text content body of a document, issue, project, initiative, project milestone, pull request, release note, AI prompt rules, or welcome message. Content is stored as a base64-encoded Yjs state and can be converted to Markdown or ProseMirror JSON. Each DocumentContent belongs to exactly one parent entity and supports real-time collaborative editing.' (DocumentContent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'DocumentContentDraft.contentState' description changed from 'The draft content state as a base64 encoded Yjs state update.' to 'The draft content state as a base64-encoded Yjs state update. This represents the user's in-progress edits that have not yet been applied to the live document.' (DocumentContentDraft.contentState)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'DocumentContentDraft.documentContent' has description 'The document content that this draft is a revision of.' (DocumentContentDraft.documentContent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'DocumentContentDraft.userId' has description 'The identifier of the user who owns this draft.' (DocumentContentDraft.userId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A draft revision of document content, pending user review.' on type 'DocumentContentDraft' has changed to 'A draft revision of document content, pending user review. Each user can have at most one draft per document content. Drafts are seeded from the live document state and stored as base64-encoded Yjs state updates, allowing independent editing without affecting the published document until the user explicitly applies their changes.' (DocumentContentDraft)

(DocumentContentHistoryType.contentData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'DocumentContentHistoryType.contentDataSnapshotAt' description changed from 'The date when the document content history snapshot was taken. This can be different than createdAt since the content is captured from its state at the previously known updatedAt timestamp in the case of an update. On document create, these timestamps can be the same.' to 'The timestamp of the document content state when this snapshot was captured. This can differ from createdAt because the content is captured from its state at the previously known updatedAt timestamp in the case of an update. On document creation, these timestamps can be identical.' (DocumentContentHistoryType.contentDataSnapshotAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'EmailIntakeAddress.address' description changed from 'Unique email address user name (before @) used for incoming email.' to 'The unique local part (before the @) of the intake email address, used to route incoming emails to the correct intake handler.'   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An email address that can be used for submitting issues.' on type 'EmailIntakeAddress' has changed to 'An email address that creates Linear issues when emails are sent to it. Email intake addresses can be scoped to a specific team or issue template, and support configurable auto-reply messages for issue creation, completion, and cancellation events. They can also be configured for the Asks web form feature, enabling external users to submit requests via email.' (EmailIntakeAddress)

Each link has a URL, display label, and sort order within its parent entity.' (EntityExternalLink)
