Can match the display name of an actual user.' to 'The external user's display name. Unique within each workspace. Can match the display name of an actual user.' (ExternalUser.displayName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ExternalUser.lastSeen' description changed from 'The last time the external user was seen interacting with Linear.' to 'The last time the external user was seen interacting with Linear through their external service. Defaults to the creation time and is updated on subsequent interactions.' (ExternalUser.lastSeen)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ExternalUser.organization' description changed from 'Organization the external user belongs to.' to 'The workspace that the external user belongs to.' (ExternalUser.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An external authenticated (e.g., through Slack) user which doesn't have a Linear account, but can create and update entities in Linear from the external system that authenticated them.' on type 'ExternalUser' has changed to 'An external user who interacts with Linear through an integrated external service (such as Slack, Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Salesforce, or Microsoft Teams) but does not have a Linear account. External users can create issues, post comments, and add reactions from their respective platforms. They are identified by service-specific user IDs and may optionally have an email address. External users are scoped to a single workspace.' (ExternalUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the facet.' to 'The sort order of the facet within its owning entity scope. Lower values appear first. Sort order is scoped by the source entity (e.g., all facets on a project are sorted independently from facets on an initiative).' (Facet.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourceFeedUser' description changed from 'The owning feed user.' to 'The user whose feed this facet belongs to. Set when this facet represents a view in a user's personal feed.' (Facet.sourceFeedUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourceInitiative' description changed from 'The owning initiative.' to 'The owning initiative. Set when this facet represents a view tab on an initiative.' (Facet.sourceInitiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourceOrganization' description changed from 'The owning organization.' to 'The owning organization. Set for workspace-level facets that are not scoped to a specific team, project, or initiative.' (Facet.sourceOrganization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourcePage' description changed from 'The owning page.' to 'The fixed page type (e.g., projects, issues) that this facet is displayed on. Used together with sourceOrganizationId or sourceTeamId to place a view on a specific page at the workspace or team level.' (Facet.sourcePage)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourceProject' description changed from 'The owning project.' to 'The owning project. Set when this facet represents a view tab on a project.' (Facet.sourceProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.sourceTeam' description changed from 'The owning team.' to 'The owning team. Set when this facet represents a view on a team-level page.' (Facet.sourceTeam)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Facet.targetCustomView' description changed from 'The targeted custom view.' to 'The custom view that this facet points to. Each facet targets exactly one custom view, establishing a one-to-one relationship.' (Facet.targetCustomView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A facet. Facets are joins between entities. A facet can tie a custom view to a project, or a a project to a roadmap for example.' on type 'Facet' has changed to 'A facet representing a join between entities. Facets connect a custom view to an owning entity such as a project, initiative, team page, workspace page, or user feed. They control the order of views within their parent via the sortOrder field. Exactly one source entity and one target entity must be set.' (Facet)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'team' was added to object type 'Favorite' (Favorite.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Favorite.owner' description changed from 'The owner of the favorite.' to 'The user who owns this favorite. Favorites are personal and only visible to their owner.' (Favorite.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Favorite.parent' description changed from 'The parent folder of the favorite.' to 'The parent folder of the favorite. Null if the favorite is at the top level of the sidebar. Only favorites of type 'folder' can be parents.' (Favorite.parent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Favorite.predefinedViewType' description changed from 'The type of favorited predefined view.' to 'The type of favorited predefined view (e.g., 'allIssues', 'activeCycle', 'backlog', 'triage'). Only populated when the favorite type is 'predefinedView'.' (Favorite.predefinedViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Favorite.sortOrder' description changed from 'The order of the item in the favorites list.' to 'The position of this item in the user's favorites list. Lower values appear first. Used to maintain user-defined ordering within the sidebar.' (Favorite.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Favorite.type' description changed from 'The type of the favorite.' to 'The type of entity this favorite references, such as 'issue', 'project', 'cycle', 'customView', 'document', 'folder', etc. Determines which associated entity field is populated.' (Favorite.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'User favorites presented in the sidebar.' on type 'Favorite' has changed to 'A user's bookmarked item that appears in their sidebar for quick access. Favorites can reference various entity types including issues, projects, cycles, views, documents, initiatives, labels, users, customers, dashboards, and pull requests. Favorites can be organized into folders and ordered by the user. Each favorite is owned by a single user and links to exactly one target entity (or is a folder containing other favorites).' (Favorite)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'FavoriteCreateInput.projectLabelId' description changed from 'The identifier of the label to favorite.' to 'The identifier of the project label to favorite.' (FavoriteCreateInput.projectLabelId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'FavoriteCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a favorite. Exactly one target entity must be specified (e.g., issueId, projectId, customViewId, folderName, etc.).' (FavoriteCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FavoritePayload.favorite' description changed from 'The object that was added as a favorite.' to 'The favorite that was created or updated.' (FavoritePayload.favorite)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'FavoritePayload' has description 'Return type for favorite mutations.' (FavoritePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'FavoriteUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating a favorite's position, parent folder, or folder name.' (FavoriteUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.initiativeUpdate' description changed from 'The initiative update that is in the feed.' to 'The initiative update referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.' (FeedItem.initiativeUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.organization' description changed from 'The organization that will see this feed item.' to 'The organization this feed item belongs to.' (FeedItem.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.post' description changed from 'The post that is in the feed.' to 'The post referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.' (FeedItem.post)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.projectUpdate' description changed from 'The project update that is in the feed.' to 'The project update referenced by this feed item. Null if the feed item references a different content type.' (FeedItem.projectUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.team' description changed from 'The team that will see this feed item.' to 'The team this feed item is associated with. Null if the feed item is not team-scoped.' (FeedItem.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FeedItem.user' description changed from 'The user that will see this feed item.' to 'The specific user this feed item is targeted to. Null if the feed item is targeted to a team or organization rather than an individual user.' (FeedItem.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] An item in a users feed.' on type 'FeedItem' has changed to '[Internal] An item in a user's activity feed. Feed items represent project updates, initiative updates, or posts that appear in a user's personalized feed based on their team memberships and feed subscriptions. Each feed item references exactly one content entity. Feed items older than 6 months are automatically archived.' (FeedItem)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FetchDataPayload.data' description changed from 'The fetched data based on the natural language query.' to 'The fetched data as a JSON object. The shape depends on the natural language query and the resolved GraphQL query. Null if the query returned no results.' (FetchDataPayload.data)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FetchDataPayload.filters' description changed from 'The filters used to fetch the data.' to 'The filter variables that were generated and applied to the GraphQL query. Null if no filters were needed.' (FetchDataPayload.filters)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'FetchDataPayload.query' description changed from 'The GraphQL query used to fetch the data.' to 'The GraphQL query that was generated from the natural language input and executed to produce the data. Useful for debugging or reusing the query directly.' (FetchDataPayload.query)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'FetchDataPayload' has description 'The result of a data fetch query using natural language.' (FetchDataPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'FrontAttachmentPayload' has description 'The result of a Front attachment mutation.' (FrontAttachmentPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationState.event' description changed from 'The event that triggers the automation.' to 'The Git event that triggers this automation rule (e.g., branch created, PR opened for review, or PR merged).' (GitAutomationState.event)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationState.state' description changed from 'The associated workflow state.' to 'The workflow state that linked issues will be transitioned to when the Git event fires. Null if this rule is configured to take no action, overriding any default rule for the same event.' (GitAutomationState.state)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationState.targetBranch' description changed from 'The target branch associated to this automation state.' to 'The target branch that this automation rule applies to. When set, this rule only fires for pull requests targeting the specified branch pattern, overriding any default rule for the same event. Null if this is a default rule that applies to all branches.' (GitAutomationState.targetBranch)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationState.team' description changed from 'The team to which this automation state belongs.' to 'The team that this automation rule belongs to. Issues must belong to this team for the automation to apply.' (GitAutomationState.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A trigger that updates the issue status according to Git automations.' on type 'GitAutomationState' has changed to 'A Git automation rule that automatically transitions issues to a specified workflow state when a Git event occurs (e.g., when a PR is opened, move the linked issue to 'In Review'). Each rule is scoped to a team and optionally to a specific target branch. When no target branch is specified, the rule acts as the default for all branches. Target-branch-specific rules override the defaults.' (GitAutomationState)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationStateCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new Git automation rule.' (GitAutomationStateCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationStatePayload' has description 'The result of a git automation state mutation.' (GitAutomationStatePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationStateUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing Git automation rule.' (GitAutomationStateUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The various states of a pull/merge request.' on type 'GitAutomationStates' has changed to 'The Git events that can trigger an automation rule. Each value corresponds to a pull/merge request lifecycle event (e.g., branch created, PR opened for review, PR merged).' (GitAutomationStates)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationTargetBranch.automationStates' description changed from 'Automation states associated with the target branch.' to 'Git automation rules associated with this target branch. These rules override the team's default automation rules when a pull request targets a branch matching this pattern.' (GitAutomationTargetBranch.automationStates)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationTargetBranch.branchPattern' description changed from 'The target branch pattern.' to 'The branch name or pattern to match against pull request target branches. Interpreted as a literal branch name unless isRegex is true, in which case it is treated as a regular expression.' (GitAutomationTargetBranch.branchPattern)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationTargetBranch.isRegex' description changed from 'Whether the branch pattern is a regular expression.' to 'Whether the branch pattern should be interpreted as a regular expression. When false, the pattern is matched as an exact branch name.' (GitAutomationTargetBranch.isRegex)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'GitAutomationTargetBranch.team' description changed from 'The team to which this Git target branch automation belongs.' to 'The team that this target branch definition belongs to. The branch pattern is unique within a team.' (GitAutomationTargetBranch.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A Git target branch for which there are automations (GitAutomationState).' on type 'GitAutomationTargetBranch' has changed to 'A target branch definition used by Git automation rules to scope automations to specific branches. The branch can be specified as an exact name (e.g., 'main') or as a regular expression pattern (e.g., 'release/.\*'). Each target branch belongs to a team and can have multiple automation rules associated with it, which override the team's default automation rules when a PR targets a matching branch.' (GitAutomationTargetBranch)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationTargetBranchCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new Git target branch definition.' (GitAutomationTargetBranchCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationTargetBranchPayload' has description 'The result of a git automation target branch mutation.' (GitAutomationTargetBranchPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'GitAutomationTargetBranchUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing Git target branch definition.' (GitAutomationTargetBranchUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'GoogleUserAccountAuthInput.inviteLink' description changed from 'An optional invite link for an organization used to populate available organizations.' to 'An optional invite link for a workspace used to populate available workspaces.' (GoogleUserAccountAuthInput.inviteLink)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IdentityProvider.samlEnabled' description changed from 'Whether SAML authentication is enabled for organization.' to 'Whether SAML authentication is enabled for the workspace.' (IdentityProvider.samlEnabled)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IdentityProvider.scimEnabled' description changed from 'Whether SCIM provisioning is enabled for organization.' to 'Whether SCIM provisioning is enabled for the workspace.' (IdentityProvider.scimEnabled)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An identity provider.' on type 'IdentityProvider' has changed to 'A SAML/SSO or web forms identity provider configuration for a workspace. Identity providers enable single sign-on authentication via SAML 2.0 and can optionally support SCIM for automated user provisioning and group-based role mapping. Each workspace can have a default general-purpose identity provider and additional web forms identity providers for Asks web authentication.' (IdentityProvider)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.completedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the initiative was moved into completed status.' to 'The time at which the initiative was moved into Completed status. Null if the initiative has not been completed.' (Initiative.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.facets' description changed from '[Internal] Facets associated with the initiative.' to '[Internal] Facets associated with the initiative, used for filtering and categorization.' (Initiative.facets)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.health' description changed from 'The health of the initiative.' to 'The overall health of the initiative, derived from the most recent initiative update. Possible values are onTrack, atRisk, or offTrack. Null if no health has been reported.' (Initiative.health)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.healthUpdatedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the initiative health was updated.' to 'The time at which the initiative health was last updated, typically when a new initiative update is posted. Null if health has never been set.' (Initiative.healthUpdatedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.icon' description changed from 'The icon of the initiative.' to 'The icon of the initiative. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon type.' (Initiative.icon)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.lastUpdate' description changed from 'The last initiative update posted for this initiative.' to 'The most recent status update posted for this initiative. Null if no updates have been posted.' (Initiative.lastUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the initiative.' to 'The workspace of the initiative.' (Initiative.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.owner' description changed from 'The user who owns the initiative.' to 'The user who owns the initiative. The owner is typically responsible for posting status updates and driving the initiative to completion. Null if no owner is assigned.' (Initiative.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.slugId' description changed from 'The initiative's unique URL slug.' to 'The initiative's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs.' (Initiative.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the initiative within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the initiative within the workspace.' (Initiative.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.startedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the initiative was moved into active status.' to 'The time at which the initiative was moved into Active status. Null if the initiative has not been activated.' (Initiative.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.status' description changed from 'The status of the initiative. One of Planned, Active, Completed' to 'The lifecycle status of the initiative. One of Planned, Active, Completed.' (Initiative.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.targetDate' description changed from 'The estimated completion date of the initiative.' to 'The estimated completion date of the initiative. Null if no target date is set.' (Initiative.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Initiative.targetDateResolution' description changed from 'The resolution of the initiative's estimated completion date.' to 'The resolution of the initiative's estimated completion date, indicating whether it refers to a specific day, week, month, quarter, or year.' (Initiative.targetDateResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An initiative to group projects.' on type 'Initiative' has changed to 'An initiative is a high-level strategic grouping of projects toward a business goal. Initiatives can contain multiple projects, have their own status updates and health tracking, and can be organized hierarchically with parent-child relationships.' (Initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the initiative within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the initiative within the workspace.' (InitiativeCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeHistory.initiative' description changed from 'The initiative that the history is associated with.' to 'The initiative that this history record belongs to.' (InitiativeHistory.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A initiative history containing relevant change events.' on type 'InitiativeHistory' has changed to 'A history record associated with an initiative. Tracks changes to initiative properties such as name, status, owner, target date, icon, color, and parent-child relationships over time.' (InitiativeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.actor' description changed from 'The user that caused the notification.' to 'The user that caused the notification. Null if the notification was triggered by a non-user actor such as an integration, external user, or system event.' (InitiativeNotification.actor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.emailedAt' description changed from 'The time at when an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null, if no email
  reminder has been sent.' to 'The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.' (InitiativeNotification.emailedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.externalUserActor' description changed from 'The external user that caused the notification.' to 'The external user that caused the notification. Populated when the notification was triggered by an external user (e.g., a commenter from a connected integration like Slack or GitHub) rather than a Linear workspace member.' (InitiativeNotification.externalUserActor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.readAt' description changed from 'The time at when the user marked the notification as read. Null, if the the user hasn't read the notification' to 'The time at which the user marked the notification as read. Null if the notification is unread.' (InitiativeNotification.readAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.snoozedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until a notification will be snoozed. After that it will appear in the inbox again.' to 'The time until which a notification is snoozed. After this time, the notification reappears in the user's inbox. Null if the notification is not currently snoozed.' (InitiativeNotification.snoozedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.type' description changed from 'Notification type.' to 'Notification type. Determines the kind of event that triggered this notification and which associated entity fields will be populated.' (InitiativeNotification.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.unsnoozedAt' description changed from 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed..' to 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed. Null if the notification has not been unsnoozed.' (InitiativeNotification.unsnoozedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotification.user' description changed from 'The user that received the notification.' to 'The recipient user of this notification.' (InitiativeNotification.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An initiative related notification.' on type 'InitiativeNotification' has changed to 'A notification related to an initiative, such as being added as owner, initiative updates, comments, or mentions.' (InitiativeNotification)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.active' description changed from 'Whether the subscription is active or not.' to 'Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.active)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.contextViewType' description changed from 'The type of view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a specific view type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.contextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.customView' description changed from 'The contextual custom view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The custom view that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.customView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.customer' description changed from 'The customer associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The customer that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.customer)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.cycle' description changed from 'The contextual cycle view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The cycle that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.cycle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.label' description changed from 'The contextual label view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.label)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes' description changed from 'The type of subscription.' to 'The notification event types that this subscription will deliver to the subscriber.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.project' description changed from 'The contextual project view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The project that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.subscriber' description changed from 'The user that subscribed to receive notifications.' to 'The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.subscriber)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.team' description changed from 'The team associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The team that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.user' description changed from 'The user view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The user that this notification subscription is scoped to, for user-specific view subscriptions. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType' description changed from 'The type of user view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An initiative notification subscription.' on type 'InitiativeNotificationSubscription' has changed to 'A notification subscription scoped to a specific initiative. The subscriber receives notifications for events related to this initiative, such as updates, comments, and ownership changes.' (InitiativeNotificationSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeRelation.initiative' description changed from 'The parent initiative.' to 'The parent initiative in this hierarchical relation.' (InitiativeRelation.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeRelation.relatedInitiative' description changed from 'The child initiative.' to 'The child initiative in this hierarchical relation.' (InitiativeRelation.relatedInitiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeRelation.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the relation within the initiative.' to 'The sort order of the child initiative within its parent initiative.' (InitiativeRelation.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeRelation.user' description changed from 'The last user who created or modified the relation.' to 'The user who last created or modified the relation. Null if the user has been deleted.' (InitiativeRelation.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A relation representing the dependency between two initiatives.' on type 'InitiativeRelation' has changed to 'A parent-child relation between two initiatives, forming a hierarchy. The initiative field is the parent and relatedInitiative is the child. Cycles and excessive nesting depth are prevented.' (InitiativeRelation)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeRelationCreateInput.initiativeId' description changed from 'The identifier of the parent initiative.' to 'The identifier of the parent initiative in the hierarchy.' (InitiativeRelationCreateInput.initiativeId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeRelationCreateInput.relatedInitiativeId' description changed from 'The identifier of the child initiative.' to 'The identifier of the child initiative in the hierarchy.' (InitiativeRelationCreateInput.relatedInitiativeId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeRelationCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the initiative relation.' to 'The sort order of the child initiative within its parent.' (InitiativeRelationCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeRelationCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a parent-child relationship between two initiatives. The initiativeId is the parent and the relatedInitiativeId is the child.' (InitiativeRelationCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeRelationPayload' has description 'The result of an initiative relation mutation.' (InitiativeRelationPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeRelationUpdateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the initiative relation.' to 'The sort order of the child initiative within its parent.' (InitiativeRelationUpdateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Join table between projects and initiatives.' on type 'InitiativeToProject' has changed to 'The join entity linking a project to an initiative. A project can only appear once in an initiative hierarchy -- it cannot be on both an initiative and one of its ancestor or descendant initiatives.' (InitiativeToProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeToProjectCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order for the project within its organization.' to 'The sort order for the project within the initiative.' (InitiativeToProjectCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeToProjectPayload.initiativeToProject' description changed from 'The initiativeToProject that was created or updated.' to 'The initiative-to-project association that was created or updated.' (InitiativeToProjectPayload.initiativeToProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The result of a initiativeToProject mutation.' on type 'InitiativeToProjectPayload' has changed to 'The result of an initiative-to-project mutation.' (InitiativeToProjectPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The properties of the initiativeToProject to update.' on type 'InitiativeToProjectUpdateInput' has changed to 'The properties of the initiative-to-project association to update.' (InitiativeToProjectUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeUpdate.health' description changed from 'The health at the time of the update.' to 'The health of the initiative at the time this update was posted. Possible values are onTrack, atRisk, or offTrack.' (InitiativeUpdate.health)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeUpdate.infoSnapshot' description changed from '[Internal] Serialized JSON representing current state of the initiative properties when posting the initiative update.' to '[Internal] A snapshot of initiative properties at the time the update was posted, including project statuses, sub-initiative health, and target dates. Used to compute diffs between consecutive updates.' (InitiativeUpdate.infoSnapshot)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeUpdate.initiative' description changed from 'The initiative that the update is associated with.' to 'The initiative that this status update was posted to.' (InitiativeUpdate.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeUpdate.isDiffHidden' description changed from 'Whether initiative update diff should be hidden.' to 'Whether the diff between this update and the previous one should be hidden in the UI.' (InitiativeUpdate.isDiffHidden)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An initiative update.' on type 'InitiativeUpdate' has changed to 'A status update posted to an initiative. Initiative updates communicate progress, health, and blockers to stakeholders. Each update captures the initiative's health at the time of writing and includes a rich-text body with the update content.' (InitiativeUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeUpdateCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new initiative update.' (InitiativeUpdateCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.frequencyResolution' description changed from 'The frequency resolution.' to 'The resolution type for the update reminder frequency (e.g., weekly, biweekly).' (InitiativeUpdateInput.frequencyResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the initiative within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the initiative within the workspace.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.targetDate' description changed from 'The estimated completion date of the initiative.' to 'The estimated completion date of the initiative. Set to null to clear.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.trashed' description changed from 'Whether the initiative has been trashed.' to 'Whether the initiative has been trashed. Set to true to trash, or null to restore.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.trashed)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequency' description changed from 'The frequency at which to prompt for updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace.' to 'The frequency at which to prompt for initiative updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace settings.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequency)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequencyInWeeks' description changed from 'The n-weekly frequency at which to prompt for updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace.' to 'The n-weekly frequency at which to prompt for initiative updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace settings.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequencyInWeeks)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.updateRemindersDay' description changed from 'The day at which to prompt for updates.' to 'The day of the week on which to prompt for initiative updates.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.updateRemindersDay)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'InitiativeUpdateInput.updateRemindersHour' description changed from 'The hour at which to prompt for updates.' to 'The hour of the day (0-23) at which to prompt for initiative updates.' (InitiativeUpdateInput.updateRemindersHour)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'InitiativeUpdatePayload.initiativeUpdate' description changed from 'The initiative update that was created.' to 'The initiative update that was created or updated.' (InitiativeUpdatePayload.initiativeUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeUpdatePayload' has description 'The result of an initiative update mutation.' (InitiativeUpdatePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeUpdateReminderPayload' has description 'The result of an initiative update reminder mutation.' (InitiativeUpdateReminderPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'InitiativeUpdateUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing initiative update.' (InitiativeUpdateUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Integration.organization' description changed from 'The organization that the integration is associated with.' to 'The workspace that the integration is associated with.' (Integration.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Integration.service' description changed from 'The integration's type.' to 'The integration's type, identifying which external service this integration connects to (e.g., 'slack', 'github', 'jira', 'figma'). This determines the shape of the integration's settings and data.' (Integration.service)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An integration with an external service.' on type 'Integration' has changed to 'An integration with an external service. Integrations connect Linear to tools like Slack, GitHub, GitLab, Jira, Figma, Sentry, Zendesk, Intercom, Front, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, Google Sheets, Microsoft Teams, Discord, Salesforce, and others. Each integration record represents a single configured connection, scoped to a workspace and optionally to a specific team, project, initiative, or custom view. Personal integrations (e.g., Slack Personal, Jira Personal, GitHub Personal) are scoped to the user who created them.' (Integration)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IntegrationRequestInput' has description 'Input for requesting a currently unavailable integration.' (IntegrationRequestInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IntegrationRequestPayload' has description 'The result of an integration request mutation.' (IntegrationRequestPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IntegrationTemplate.foreignEntityId' description changed from 'ID of the foreign entity in the external integration this template is for, e.g., Slack channel ID.' to 'The identifier of the foreign entity in the external service that this template is scoped to. For example, a Slack channel ID indicating which channel should use this template for creating issues. When null, the template applies to the integration as a whole rather than a specific external resource.' (IntegrationTemplate.foreignEntityId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Join table between templates and integrations.' on type 'IntegrationTemplate' has changed to 'A connection between a template and an integration. This join entity links Linear issue templates to integrations so that external systems (e.g., Slack Asks channels) can use specific templates when creating issues. Each record optionally includes a foreign entity ID to scope the template to a specific external resource, such as a particular Slack channel.' (IntegrationTemplate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IntegrationTemplateCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new integration template.' (IntegrationTemplateCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IntegrationTemplatePayload' has description 'The result of an integration template mutation.' (IntegrationTemplatePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'microsoftTeamsProjectUpdateCreated' was added to object type 'IntegrationsSettings' (IntegrationsSettings.microsoftTeamsProjectUpdateCreated)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IntegrationsSettings.slackInitiativeUpdateCreated' description changed from 'Whether to send a Slack message when a initiate update is created.' to 'Whether to send a Slack message when an initiative update is created.' (IntegrationsSettings.slackInitiativeUpdateCreated)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The configuration of all integrations for different entities.' on type 'IntegrationsSettings' has changed to 'The configuration of all integrations for different entities. Controls Slack notification preferences for a specific team, project, initiative, or custom view. Exactly one of teamId, projectId, customViewId, or initiativeId must be set, determining which entity these integration settings apply to.' (IntegrationsSettings)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.assignee' description changed from 'The user to whom the issue is assigned to.' to 'The user to whom the issue is assigned. Null if the issue is unassigned.' (Issue.assignee)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.creator' description changed from 'The user who created the issue.' to 'The user who created the issue. Null if the creator's account has been deleted or if the issue was created by an integration or system process.' (Issue.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.cycle' description changed from 'The cycle that the issue is associated with.' to 'The cycle that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not part of any cycle.' (Issue.cycle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.delegate' description changed from 'The agent user that is delegated to work on this issue.' to 'The agent user that is delegated to work on this issue. Set when an AI agent has been assigned to perform work on this issue. Null if no agent is working on the issue.' (Issue.delegate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.estimate' description changed from 'The estimate of the complexity of the issue..' to 'The estimate of the complexity of the issue. The specific scale used depends on the team's estimation configuration (e.g., points, T-shirt sizes). Null if no estimate has been set.' (Issue.estimate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.externalUserCreator' description changed from 'The external user who created the issue.' to 'The external user who created the issue. Set when the issue was created via an integration (e.g., Slack, Intercom) on behalf of a non-Linear user. Null if the issue was created by a Linear user.' (Issue.externalUserCreator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.labelIds' description changed from 'Id of the labels associated with this issue.' to 'Identifiers of the labels associated with this issue. Can be used to query the labels directly.' (Issue.labelIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.number' description changed from 'The issue's unique number.' to 'The issue's unique number, scoped to the issue's team. Together with the team key, this forms the issue's human-readable identifier (e.g., ENG-123).' (Issue.number)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.prioritySortOrder' description changed from 'The order of the item in relation to other items in the organization, when ordered by priority.' to 'The order of the item in relation to other items in the workspace, when ordered by priority.' (Issue.prioritySortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.project' description changed from 'The project that the issue is associated with.' to 'The project that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not part of any project.' (Issue.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.projectMilestone' description changed from 'The projectMilestone that the issue is associated with.' to 'The project milestone that the issue is associated with. Null if the issue is not assigned to a specific milestone within its project.' (Issue.projectMilestone)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.reactionData' description changed from 'Emoji reaction summary, grouped by emoji type.' to 'Emoji reaction summary for the issue, grouped by emoji type. Contains the count and reacting user information for each emoji.' (Issue.reactionData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.sortOrder' description changed from 'The order of the item in relation to other items in the organization.' to 'The order of the item in relation to other items in the organization. Used for manual sorting in list views.' (Issue.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.sourceComment' description changed from 'The comment that this issue was created from.' to 'The comment that this issue was created from, when an issue is created from an existing comment. Null if the issue was not created from a comment.' (Issue.sourceComment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.state' description changed from 'The workflow state that the issue is associated with.' to 'The workflow state (issue status) that the issue is currently in. Workflow states represent the issue's progress through the team's workflow, such as Triage, Todo, In Progress, Done, or Canceled.' (Issue.state)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.team' description changed from 'The team that the issue is associated with.' to 'The team that the issue belongs to. Every issue must belong to exactly one team, which determines the available workflow states, labels, and other team-specific configuration.' (Issue.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Issue.title' description changed from 'The issue's title.' to 'The issue's title. This is the primary human-readable summary of the work item.' (Issue.title)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An issue.' on type 'Issue' has changed to 'An issue is the core work item in Linear. Issues belong to a team, have a workflow status, can be assigned to users, carry a priority level, and can be organized into projects and cycles. Issues support sub-issues (parent-child hierarchy up to 10 levels deep), labels, due dates, estimates, and SLA tracking. They can also be linked to other issues via relations, attached to releases, and tracked through their full history of changes.' (Issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueBatchCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating multiple issues at once in a batch operation. Up to 50 issues can be created in a single batch.' (IssueBatchCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueBatchPayload' has description 'The result of a batch issue mutation, containing the updated issues and a success indicator.' (IssueBatchPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'IssueCreateInput.completedAt' description changed from 'The date when the issue was completed (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a date in the past and after createdAt date. Cannot be provided with an incompatible workflow state.' to 'The time at which the issue was completed (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past and after createdAt. Cannot be provided with an incompatible workflow state.' (IssueCreateInput.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'IssueCreateInput.createdAt' description changed from 'The date when the issue was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a date in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.' to 'The time at which the issue was created (e.g. if importing from another system). Must be a time in the past. If none is provided, the backend will generate the time as now.' (IssueCreateInput.createdAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'IssueCreateInput.slaBreachesAt' description changed from '[Internal] The timestamp at which an issue will be considered in breach of SLA.' to '[Internal] The time at which an issue will be considered in breach of SLA.' (IssueCreateInput.slaBreachesAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'IssueCreateInput.slaStartedAt' description changed from '[Internal] The timestamp at which the issue's SLA was started.' to '[Internal] The time at which the issue's SLA was started.' (IssueCreateInput.slaStartedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new issue. At minimum, a team must be specified. A title is required unless a template is provided. All other fields are optional and will use defaults from the team or template if not specified.' (IssueCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.assigneeId' description changed from 'The user assigned to the draft.' to 'Identifier of the user assigned to the draft. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the draft is unassigned.' (IssueDraft.assigneeId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.cycleId' description changed from 'The cycle associated with the draft.' to 'Identifier of the cycle associated with the draft. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if no cycle is assigned.' (IssueDraft.cycleId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.estimate' description changed from 'The estimate of the complexity of the draft.' to 'The estimate of the complexity of the draft. Null if no estimate has been set.' (IssueDraft.estimate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.labelIds' description changed from 'The IDs of labels added to the draft.' to 'Identifiers of the labels added to the draft. These labels will be applied to the issue when the draft is published.' (IssueDraft.labelIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.parent' description changed from 'The parent draft of the draft.' to 'The parent draft of the draft. Set when this draft represents a sub-issue of another draft. Null if this is a top-level draft or has a parent issue instead.' (IssueDraft.parent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.parentIssue' description changed from 'The parent issue of the draft.' to 'The parent issue of the draft. Set when this draft represents a sub-issue that will be created under an existing issue. Null if this is a top-level draft or has a parent draft instead.' (IssueDraft.parentIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.priority' description changed from 'The priority of the draft.' to 'The priority of the draft. 0 = No priority, 1 = Urgent, 2 = High, 3 = Normal, 4 = Low.' (IssueDraft.priority)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.projectId' description changed from 'The project associated with the draft.' to 'Identifier of the project associated with the draft. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if no project is assigned.' (IssueDraft.projectId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.projectMilestoneId' description changed from 'The project milestone associated with the draft.' to 'Identifier of the project milestone associated with the draft. Can be used to query the project milestone directly. Null if no milestone is assigned.' (IssueDraft.projectMilestoneId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.releaseIds' description changed from 'The IDs of releases associated with the draft.' to 'Identifiers of the releases associated with the draft. These releases will be linked to the issue when the draft is published.' (IssueDraft.releaseIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.sourceCommentId' description changed from 'The ID of the comment that the draft was created from.' to 'Identifier of the comment that the draft was created from. Set when a draft is created from an existing comment. Null if the draft was not created from a comment.' (IssueDraft.sourceCommentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.stateId' description changed from 'The workflow state associated with the draft.' to 'Identifier of the workflow state associated with the draft. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Determines the initial status the issue will have when the draft is published.' (IssueDraft.stateId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.teamId' description changed from 'The team associated with the draft.' to 'Identifier of the team associated with the draft. Can be used to query the team directly.' (IssueDraft.teamId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueDraft.title' description changed from 'The draft's title.' to 'The draft's title. This will become the issue's title when the draft is published.' (IssueDraft.title)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A draft issue.' on type 'IssueDraft' has changed to '[Internal] A draft issue that has not yet been created as a full issue. Drafts allow users to prepare issue details (title, description, labels, assignee, etc.) before committing them. Drafts belong to a team and a creator, and support a parent-child hierarchy similar to issues. A draft can have either a parent draft or a parent issue, but not both.' (IssueDraft)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueFilterSuggestionPayload' has description 'The result of an AI-generated issue filter suggestion based on a text prompt.' (IssueFilterSuggestionPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.actorId' description changed from 'The id of user who made these changes. If null, possibly means that the change made by an integration.' to 'Identifier of the user who made these changes. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the change was made by an integration, automation, or system process.' (IssueHistory.actorId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.archived' description changed from 'Whether the issue is archived at the time of this history entry.' to 'Whether the issue was archived (true) or unarchived (false) in this change. Null if the archive status was not changed.' (IssueHistory.archived)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.attachmentId' description changed from 'The id of linked attachment.' to 'Identifier of the attachment that was linked to or unlinked from the issue. Can be used to query the attachment directly. Null if no attachment change occurred.' (IssueHistory.attachmentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.customerNeedId' description changed from 'The id of linked customer need.' to '[Deprecated] Identifier of the customer need that was linked to the issue. Use customer need related arrays in changes instead.' (IssueHistory.customerNeedId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromAssigneeId' description changed from 'The id of user from whom the issue was re-assigned from.' to 'Identifier of the user from whom the issue was re-assigned. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the assignee was not changed or the issue was previously unassigned.' (IssueHistory.fromAssigneeId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromCycleId' description changed from 'The id of previous cycle of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the previous cycle of the issue. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if the cycle was not changed or the issue was not in a cycle.' (IssueHistory.fromCycleId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromParentId' description changed from 'The id of previous parent of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the previous parent issue. Can be used to query the issue directly. Null if the parent was not changed or the issue previously had no parent.' (IssueHistory.fromParentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromProjectId' description changed from 'The id of previous project of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the previous project of the issue. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the project was not changed or the issue was not in a project.' (IssueHistory.fromProjectId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromStateId' description changed from 'The id of previous workflow state of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the previous workflow state (issue status) of the issue. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Null if the status was not changed.' (IssueHistory.fromStateId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.fromTeamId' description changed from 'The id of team from which the issue was moved from.' to 'Identifier of the team from which the issue was moved. Can be used to query the team directly. Null if the team was not changed.' (IssueHistory.fromTeamId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toAssigneeId' description changed from 'The id of user to whom the issue was assigned to.' to 'Identifier of the user to whom the issue was assigned. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the assignee was not changed or the issue was unassigned.' (IssueHistory.toAssigneeId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toConvertedProjectId' description changed from 'The id of new project created from the issue.' to 'Identifier of the new project that was created by converting this issue to a project. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the issue was not converted to a project.' (IssueHistory.toConvertedProjectId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toCycleId' description changed from 'The id of new cycle of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the new cycle of the issue. Can be used to query the cycle directly. Null if the cycle was not changed or the issue was removed from a cycle.' (IssueHistory.toCycleId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toParentId' description changed from 'The id of new parent of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the new parent issue. Can be used to query the issue directly. Null if the parent was not changed or the issue was removed from its parent.' (IssueHistory.toParentId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toProjectId' description changed from 'The id of new project of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the new project of the issue. Can be used to query the project directly. Null if the project was not changed or the issue was removed from a project.' (IssueHistory.toProjectId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toStateId' description changed from 'The id of new workflow state of the issue.' to 'Identifier of the new workflow state (issue status) of the issue. Can be used to query the workflow state directly. Null if the status was not changed.' (IssueHistory.toStateId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueHistory.toTeamId' description changed from 'The id of team to which the issue was moved to.' to 'Identifier of the team to which the issue was moved. Can be used to query the team directly. Null if the team was not changed.' (IssueHistory.toTeamId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A record of changes to an issue.' on type 'IssueHistory' has changed to 'A record of changes to an issue. Each history entry captures one or more property changes made to an issue within a short grouping window by the same actor. History entries track changes to fields such as title, assignee, status, priority, project, cycle, labels, due date, estimate, parent issue, and more. They also record metadata about what triggered the change (e.g., a user action, workflow automation, triage rule, or integration).' (IssueHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An error that occurred during triage rule execution.' on type 'IssueHistoryTriageRuleError' has changed to 'An error that occurred during triage rule execution, such as a conflicting label assignment or an invalid property value. Contains the error type and optionally the property that caused the failure.' (IssueHistoryTriageRuleError)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Metadata about a triage rule that made changes to an issue.' on type 'IssueHistoryTriageRuleMetadata' has changed to 'Metadata about a triage responsibility rule that made changes to an issue, such as auto-assigning an issue when it enters triage. Includes information about any errors that occurred during rule execution.' (IssueHistoryTriageRuleMetadata)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Metadata about a workflow that made changes to an issue.' on type 'IssueHistoryWorkflowMetadata' has changed to 'Metadata about a workflow automation that made changes to an issue. Links the issue history entry back to the workflow definition that triggered the change, and optionally to any AI conversation involved in the automation.' (IssueHistoryWorkflowMetadata)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImport.creatorId' description changed from 'The id for the user that started the job.' to 'Identifier of the user who started the import job. Can be used to query the user directly. Null if the user has been deleted.' (IssueImport.creatorId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImport.progress' description changed from 'Current step progress in % (0-100).' to 'Current step progress as a percentage (0-100). Null if the import has not yet started or progress tracking is not available.' (IssueImport.progress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImport.service' description changed from 'The service from which data will be imported.' to 'The external service from which data is being imported (e.g., jira, asana, github, shortcut, linear).' (IssueImport.service)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImport.status' description changed from 'The status for the import job.' to 'The current status of the import job, indicating its position in the import lifecycle (e.g., not started, in progress, complete, error).' (IssueImport.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImport.teamName' description changed from 'New team's name in cases when teamId not set.' to 'The name of the new team to be created for the import, when the import is configured to create a new team rather than importing into an existing one. Null if importing into an existing team.' (IssueImport.teamName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An import job for data from an external service.' on type 'IssueImport' has changed to 'An import job for data from an external service such as Jira, Asana, GitHub, Shortcut, or other project management tools. Import jobs track the full lifecycle of importing issues, labels, workflow states, and other data into a Linear workspace, including progress, status, error states, and data mapping configuration.' (IssueImport)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueImportCheckPayload' has description 'The result of checking whether an import from an external service can proceed.' (IssueImportCheckPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueImportDeletePayload' has description 'The result of deleting an issue import, containing the deleted import job and a success indicator.' (IssueImportDeletePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImportJqlCheckPayload.count' description changed from 'Returns an approximate number of issues matching the JQL query, if available' to 'An approximate number of issues matching the JQL query. Null when the query is invalid or the count is unavailable.' (IssueImportJqlCheckPayload.count)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Whether a custom JQL query is valid or not' on type 'IssueImportJqlCheckPayload' has changed to 'The result of validating a custom JQL query for a Jira import.' (IssueImportJqlCheckPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueImportPayload' has description 'The result of an issue import mutation, containing the import job and a success indicator.' (IssueImportPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueImportSyncCheckPayload.error' description changed from 'An error message with a root cause of why the import cannot be synced' to 'An error message explaining why the import cannot be synced. Null when canSync is true.' (IssueImportSyncCheckPayload.error)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Whether an issue import can be synced at the end of an import or not' on type 'IssueImportSyncCheckPayload' has changed to 'The result of checking whether an issue import can be synced with its source service after import completes.' (IssueImportSyncCheckPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueImportUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an import job's mapping configuration, such as user and workflow state mappings between the source service and Linear.' (IssueImportUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.children' description changed from 'Children of the label.' to 'Child labels within this label group. Only populated when the label is a group (isGroup is true).' (IssueLabel.children)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.color' description changed from 'The label's color as a HEX string.' to 'The label's color as a HEX string (e.g., '#EB5757'). Used for visual identification of the label in the UI.' (IssueLabel.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.inheritedFrom' description changed from 'The original label inherited from.' to 'The original workspace or parent-team label that this label was inherited from. Null if the label is not inherited.' (IssueLabel.inheritedFrom)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.isGroup' description changed from 'Whether the label is a group.' to 'Whether the label is a group. When true, this label acts as a container for child labels and cannot be directly applied to issues or projects. When false, the label can be directly applied.' (IssueLabel.isGroup)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.lastAppliedAt' description changed from 'The date when the label was last applied to an issue or project.' to 'The date when the label was last applied to an issue or project. Null if the label has never been applied.' (IssueLabel.lastAppliedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueLabel.team' description changed from 'The team that the label is associated with. If null, the label is associated with the global workspace.' to 'The team that the label is scoped to. If null, the label is a workspace-level label available to all teams in the workspace.' (IssueLabel.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Labels that can be associated with issues.' on type 'IssueLabel' has changed to 'Labels that can be associated with issues. Labels help categorize and filter issues across a workspace. They can be workspace-level (shared across all teams) or team-scoped. Labels have a color for visual identification and can be organized hierarchically into groups, where a parent label acts as a group containing child labels. Labels may also be inherited from parent teams to sub-teams.' (IssueLabel)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'IssueLabelCreateInput.retiredAt' description changed from 'When the label was retired.' to 'The time at which the label was retired. Set to null to restore a retired label.' (IssueLabelCreateInput.retiredAt)

  reminder has been sent.' to 'The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.' (IssueNotification.emailedAt)

(IssueNotification.externalUserActor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A relation between two issues.' on type 'IssueRelation' has changed to 'A relation between two issues. Issue relations represent directional relationships such as blocking, being blocked by, relating to, or duplicating another issue. Each relation connects a source issue to a related issue with a specific type describing the nature of the relationship.' (IssueRelation)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueRelationCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new issue relation between two issues. Both the source issue and related issue must be specified along with the relationship type.' (IssueRelationCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.dismissalReason' has description '[Internal] The reason the suggestion was dismissed by the user. Null if the suggestion has not been dismissed.' (IssueSuggestion.dismissalReason)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.issue' has description '[Internal] The issue that this suggestion applies to.' (IssueSuggestion.issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.issueId' has description '[Internal] Identifier of the issue that this suggestion applies to. Can be used to query the issue directly.' (IssueSuggestion.issueId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.metadata' has description '[Internal] Metadata associated with the suggestion, including confidence scores and classification. Null if no metadata is available.' (IssueSuggestion.metadata)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.stateChangedAt' has description '[Internal] The date when the suggestion's state was last changed (e.g., from active to accepted or dismissed).' (IssueSuggestion.stateChangedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedIssue' has description '[Internal] The suggested issue, when the suggestion type is similarIssue or relatedIssue. Null for other suggestion types.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedIssueId' has description '[Internal] Identifier of the suggested issue. Set when the suggestion type is similarIssue or relatedIssue. Can be used to query the issue directly.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedIssueId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedLabel' has description '[Internal] The suggested label, when the suggestion type is label. Null for other suggestion types.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedLabel)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedLabelId' has description '[Internal] Identifier of the suggested label. Set when the suggestion type is label. Can be used to query the label directly.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedLabelId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedProject' has description '[Internal] The suggested project, when the suggestion type is project. Null for other suggestion types.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedTeam' has description '[Internal] The suggested team, when the suggestion type is team. Null for other suggestion types.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedTeam)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedUser' has description '[Internal] The suggested user, when the suggestion type is assignee. Null for other suggestion types.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestion.suggestedUserId' has description '[Internal] Identifier of the suggested user. Set when the suggestion type is assignee. Can be used to query the user directly.' (IssueSuggestion.suggestedUserId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'IssueSuggestion' has description '[Internal] An AI-generated suggestion for an issue.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestionMetadata.rank' has description '[Internal] The rank of this suggestion relative to other suggestions of the same type. Lower values indicate higher priority. Null if not ranked.' (IssueSuggestionMetadata.rank)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestionMetadata.reasons' has description '[Internal] The reasons explaining why this suggestion was made. Null if no reasons are available.' (IssueSuggestionMetadata.reasons)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'IssueSuggestionMetadata.score' has description '[Internal] The confidence score of the suggestion. Higher values indicate greater confidence. Null if no score was computed.' (IssueSuggestionMetadata.score)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'sourceCustomerId' on field 'Mutation.customerMerge' changed from 'The ID of the customer to merge.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.customerNeedUpdate' description changed from 'Updates a customer need' to 'Updates an existing customer need.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.customerStatusDelete' description changed from 'Deletes a customer status.' to 'Deletes a customer status. Cannot delete the last remaining status in a workspace, and the status must not be in use by any customers.' (Mutation.customerStatusDelete)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Mutation.customerTierDelete' description changed from 'Deletes a customer tier.' to 'Deletes a customer tier. The tier must not be in use by any customers.' (Mutation.customerTierDelete)
