  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.cancelAt' description changed from 'The date the subscription is scheduled to be canceled, if any.' to 'The date the subscription is scheduled to be canceled in the future. Null if no cancellation is scheduled. The subscription remains active until this date.' (PaidSubscription.cancelAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.canceledAt' description changed from 'The date the subscription was canceled, if any.' to 'The date the subscription was canceled. Null if the subscription has not been canceled.' (PaidSubscription.canceledAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.collectionMethod' description changed from 'The collection method for this subscription, either automatically charged or invoiced.' to 'The billing collection method for this subscription. 'automatic' means the payment method on file is charged automatically. 'send_invoice' means invoices are sent to the billing email for manual payment.' (PaidSubscription.collectionMethod)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.creator' description changed from 'The creator of the subscription.' to 'The user who initially created (purchased) the subscription. Null if the creator has been removed from the workspace.' (PaidSubscription.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.nextBillingAt' description changed from 'The date the subscription will be billed next.' to 'The date the subscription will be billed next. Null if the subscription is canceled or has no upcoming billing date.' (PaidSubscription.nextBillingAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.organization' description changed from 'The organization that the subscription is associated with.' to 'The workspace that the subscription is associated with.' (PaidSubscription.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.pendingChangeType' description changed from 'The subscription type of a pending change. Null if no change pending.' to 'The subscription plan type that the workspace is scheduled to change to at the next billing cycle. Null if no plan change is pending.' (PaidSubscription.pendingChangeType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.seats' description changed from 'The number of seats in the subscription.' to 'The number of seats (active members) in the subscription. This is the raw count before applying minimum and maximum seat limits.' (PaidSubscription.seats)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.seatsMaximum' description changed from 'The maximum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription.' to 'The maximum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription. The billed seat count will never exceed this value even if actual member count is higher. Null if no maximum is enforced.' (PaidSubscription.seatsMaximum)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.seatsMinimum' description changed from 'The minimum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription.' to 'The minimum number of seats that will be billed in the subscription. The billed seat count will never go below this value even if actual member count is lower. Null if no minimum is enforced.' (PaidSubscription.seatsMinimum)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PaidSubscription.type' description changed from 'The subscription type.' to 'The subscription plan type (e.g., basic, business, enterprise). Determines the feature set and pricing tier for the workspace.' (PaidSubscription.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The paid subscription of an organization.' on type 'PaidSubscription' has changed to 'The billing subscription of a workspace. Represents an active paid plan (e.g., Basic, Business, Enterprise) backed by a Stripe subscription. If a workspace has no Subscription record, it is on the free plan. Only one active subscription per workspace is expected.' (PaidSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PasskeyLoginStartResponse.options' has description 'The passkey authentication options to pass to the WebAuthn API.' (PasskeyLoginStartResponse.options)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PasskeyLoginStartResponse.success' has description 'Whether the operation was successful.' (PasskeyLoginStartResponse.success)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.audioSummary' description changed from 'The update content summarized for audio consumption.' to 'The post content summarized for audio text-to-speech consumption. Null if no audio summary has been generated.' (Post.audioSummary)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.body' description changed from 'The update content in markdown format.' to 'The post content in markdown format.' (Post.body)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.bodyData' description changed from '[Internal] The content of the post as a Prosemirror document.' to '[Internal] The content of the post as a ProseMirror document. This is the canonical rich-text representation of the post body.' (Post.bodyData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.creator' description changed from 'The user who wrote the post.' to 'The user who created the post. Null for system-generated posts.' (Post.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.editedAt' description changed from 'The time the post was edited.' to 'The time the post was last edited. Null if the post has not been edited since creation.' (Post.editedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.evalLogId' description changed from 'The log id of the ai response.' to 'The evaluation log ID of the AI response that generated this post. Null for non-AI-generated posts.' (Post.evalLogId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.feedSummaryScheduleAtCreate' description changed from 'Schedule used to create a post summary.' to 'The feed summary schedule cadence that was active when this post was created. Null for non-summary posts.' (Post.feedSummaryScheduleAtCreate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.reactionData' description changed from 'Emoji reaction summary, grouped by emoji type.' to 'Emoji reaction summary for this post, grouped by emoji type. Each entry contains the emoji name, count, and the IDs of users who reacted.' (Post.reactionData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.slugId' description changed from 'The post's unique URL slug.' to 'The post's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs.' (Post.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.team' description changed from 'The team that the post is associated with.' to 'The team that the post is scoped to, for team-level feed posts. Null for user-scoped posts.' (Post.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.title' description changed from 'The post's title.' to 'The post's title. Null or empty for posts that do not have a title.' (Post.title)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.ttlUrl' description changed from 'A URL of the TTL (text-to-language) for the body.' to 'A URL of the text-to-speech audio rendering of the post body. Null if no audio has been generated.' (Post.ttlUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.type' description changed from 'The type of the post.' to 'The type of the post, such as 'summary' for AI-generated feed summaries. Null for standard user-authored posts.' (Post.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.user' description changed from 'The user that the post is associated with.' to 'The user that the post is scoped to, for user-level feed posts. Null for team-scoped posts.' (Post.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Post.writtenSummaryData' description changed from '[Internal] The written update data used to compose the written post.' to '[Internal] The structured data used to compose an AI-generated written summary post, including section content and source references.' (Post.writtenSummaryData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A generic post.' on type 'Post' has changed to '[Internal] A post or announcement in a team or user feed. Posts can be manually authored by users or AI-generated summaries of team activity. They support rich text content (ProseMirror), emoji reactions, threaded comments, and audio summaries. Posts are associated with either a team or a user, but not both.' (Post)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.actor' description changed from 'The user that caused the notification.' to 'The user that caused the notification. Null if the notification was triggered by a non-user actor such as an integration, external user, or system event.' (PostNotification.actor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.emailedAt' description changed from 'The time at when an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null, if no email
  reminder has been sent.' to 'The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.' (PostNotification.emailedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.externalUserActor' description changed from 'The external user that caused the notification.' to 'The external user that caused the notification. Populated when the notification was triggered by an external user (e.g., a commenter from a connected integration like Slack or GitHub) rather than a Linear workspace member.' (PostNotification.externalUserActor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.readAt' description changed from 'The time at when the user marked the notification as read.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.snoozedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until a notification will be snoozed. Null if the notification is not currently snoozed.' (PostNotification.snoozedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.type' description changed from 'Notification type.' to 'Notification type. Determines the kind of event that triggered this notification and which associated entity fields will be populated.' (PostNotification.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.unsnoozedAt' description changed from 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed..' to 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed. Null if the notification has not been unsnoozed.' (PostNotification.unsnoozedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PostNotification.user' description changed from 'The user that received the notification.' to 'The recipient user of this notification.' (PostNotification.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A post related notification.' on type 'PostNotification' has changed to 'A notification related to a post, such as new comments or reactions.' (PostNotification)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.autoArchivedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the project was automatically archived by the auto pruning process.' to 'The time at which the project was automatically archived by the auto-pruning process.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.canceledAt' description changed from 'The time at which the project was moved into canceled state.' to 'The time at which the project was moved into a canceled status. Null if the project has not been canceled.' (Project.canceledAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.color' description changed from 'The project's color.' to 'The project's color as a HEX string. Used in the UI to visually identify the project.' (Project.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.completedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the project was moved into completed state.' to 'The time at which the project was moved into a completed status. Null if the project has not been completed.' (Project.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.completedIssueCountHistory' description changed from 'The number of completed issues in the project after each week.' to 'The number of completed issues in the project at the end of each week since project creation.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.completedScopeHistory' description changed from 'The number of completed estimation points after each week.' to 'The number of completed estimation points at the end of each week since project creation. Each entry represents one week.' (Project.completedScopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.convertedFromIssue' description changed from 'The project was created based on this issue.' to 'The issue that was converted into this project. Null if the project was not created from an issue.' (Project.convertedFromIssue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.currentProgress' description changed from '[INTERNAL] The current progress of the project.' to '[INTERNAL] The current progress of the project, broken down by issue status category.' (Project.currentProgress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.description' description changed from 'The project's description.' to 'The short description of the project.' (Project.description)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.facets' description changed from '[Internal] Facets associated with the project.' to '[Internal] Facets associated with the project, used for filtering and categorization.' (Project.facets)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.health' description changed from 'The health of the project.' to 'The overall health of the project, derived from the most recent project update. Possible values are onTrack, atRisk, or offTrack. Null if no health has been reported.' (Project.health)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.healthUpdatedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the project health was updated.' to 'The time at which the project health was last updated, typically when a new project update is posted. Null if health has never been set.' (Project.healthUpdatedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.icon' description changed from 'The icon of the project.' to 'The icon of the project. Can be an emoji or a decorative icon type.' (Project.icon)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.inProgressScopeHistory' description changed from 'The number of in progress estimation points after each week.' to 'The number of in-progress estimation points at the end of each week since project creation. Each entry represents one week.' (Project.inProgressScopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.issueCountHistory' description changed from 'The total number of issues in the project after each week.' to 'The total number of issues in the project at the end of each week since project creation. Each entry represents one week.' (Project.issueCountHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.labelIds' description changed from 'Id of the labels associated with this project.' to 'The IDs of the project labels associated with this project.' (Project.labelIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.lastUpdate' description changed from 'The last project update posted for this project.' to 'The most recent status update posted for this project. Null if no updates have been posted.' (Project.lastUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.lead' description changed from 'The project lead.' to 'The user who leads the project. The project lead is typically responsible for posting status updates and driving the project to completion. Null if no lead is assigned.' (Project.lead)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.name' description changed from 'The project's name.' to 'The name of the project.' (Project.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.prioritySortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order for the project within the organization, when ordered by priority.' to 'The sort order for the project within the workspace when ordered by priority.' (Project.prioritySortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.progressHistory' description changed from '[INTERNAL] The progress history of the project.' to '[INTERNAL] The progress history of the project, tracking issue completion over time.' (Project.progressHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.projectUpdateRemindersPausedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until which project update reminders are paused.' to 'The time until which project update reminders are paused. When set, no update reminders will be sent for this project until this date passes. Null means reminders are active.' (Project.projectUpdateRemindersPausedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.scopeHistory' description changed from 'The total number of estimation points after each week.' to 'The total scope (estimation points) of the project at the end of each week since project creation. Each entry represents one week.' (Project.scopeHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.slugId' description changed from 'The project's unique URL slug.' to 'The project's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs.' (Project.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order for the project within the organization.' to 'The sort order for the project within the workspace. Used for manual ordering in list views.' (Project.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.startDate' description changed from 'The estimated start date of the project.' to 'The estimated start date of the project. Null if no start date is set.' (Project.startDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.startDateResolution' description changed from 'The resolution of the project's start date.' to 'The resolution of the project's start date, indicating whether it refers to a specific month, quarter, half-year, or year.' (Project.startDateResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.startedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the project was moved into started state.' to 'The time at which the project was moved into a started status. Null if the project has not been started.' (Project.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.status' description changed from 'The status that the project is associated with.' to 'The current project status. Defines the project's position in its lifecycle (e.g., backlog, planned, started, paused, completed, canceled).' (Project.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.targetDate' description changed from 'The estimated completion date of the project.' to 'The estimated completion date of the project. Null if no target date is set.' (Project.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Project.targetDateResolution' description changed from 'The resolution of the project's estimated completion date.' to 'The resolution of the project's estimated completion date, indicating whether it refers to a specific month, quarter, half-year, or year.' (Project.targetDateResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A project.' on type 'Project' has changed to 'A project is a collection of issues working toward a shared goal. Projects have start and target dates, milestones, status tracking, and progress metrics. They can span multiple teams and be grouped under initiatives.' (Project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectAttachment.metadata' description changed from 'Custom metadata related to the attachment.' to 'Custom metadata related to the attachment. Contains user-facing content such as conversation messages or rendered attributes from integrations.' (ProjectAttachment.metadata)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectAttachment.source' description changed from 'Information about the external source which created the attachment.' to 'Metadata about the external source which created the attachment, including foreign identifiers used for syncing with external services. Null if the attachment was not created by an integration.' (ProjectAttachment.source)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectAttachment.sourceType' description changed from 'An accessor helper to source.type, defines the source type of the attachment.' to 'The source type of the attachment, derived from the source metadata. Returns the integration type that created the attachment (e.g., 'slack', 'github'), or null if not set.' (ProjectAttachment.sourceType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectAttachment.subtitle' description changed from 'Optional subtitle of the attachment' to 'Optional subtitle of the attachment, providing additional context below the title.' (ProjectAttachment.subtitle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Project attachment' on type 'ProjectAttachment' has changed to 'An attachment (link, reference, or integration data) associated with a project. Attachments are typically created by integrations and contain metadata for rendering in the client.' (ProjectAttachment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectCreateInput.convertedFromIssueId' description changed from 'The ID of the issue from which that project is created.' to 'The ID of the issue that was converted into this project.' (ProjectCreateInput.convertedFromIssueId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectCreateInput.labelIds' description changed from '[Internal]The identifiers of the project labels associated with this project.' to '[Internal] The identifiers of the project labels associated with this project.' (ProjectCreateInput.labelIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectCreateInput.templateId' description changed from 'The ID of the template to apply when creating the project.' to 'The ID of a project template to apply when creating the project. Overrides useDefaultTemplate if both are provided.' (ProjectCreateInput.templateId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new project. A name and at least one team are required. All other fields are optional and will use defaults if not specified.' (ProjectCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectFilterSuggestionPayload' has description 'The result of a project filter suggestion query.' (ProjectFilterSuggestionPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectHistory.project' description changed from 'The project that the history is associated with.' to 'The project that this history record belongs to.' (ProjectHistory.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An history associated with a project.' on type 'ProjectHistory' has changed to 'A history record associated with a project. Tracks changes to project properties, status, members, teams, milestones, labels, and relationships over time.' (ProjectHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.color' description changed from 'The label's color as a HEX string.' to 'The label's color as a HEX string (e.g., '#EB5757'). Used for visual identification of the label in the UI.' (ProjectLabel.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.isGroup' description changed from 'Whether the label is a group.' to 'Whether the label is a group. When true, this label acts as a container for child labels and cannot be directly applied to issues or projects. When false, the label can be directly applied.' (ProjectLabel.isGroup)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.lastAppliedAt' description changed from 'The date when the label was last applied to an issue or project.' to 'The date when the label was last applied to an issue or project. Null if the label has never been applied.' (ProjectLabel.lastAppliedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.organization' has description 'The workspace that the project label belongs to.' (ProjectLabel.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.parent' description changed from 'The parent label.' to 'The parent label group. If set, this label is a child within a group. Only one child label from each group can be applied to a project at a time.' (ProjectLabel.parent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectLabel.retiredBy' description changed from 'The user who retired the label.' to 'The user who retired the label. Retired labels cannot be applied to new projects but remain on existing ones. Null if the label is active.' (ProjectLabel.retiredBy)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Labels that can be associated with projects.' on type 'ProjectLabel' has changed to 'A label that can be applied to projects for categorization. Project labels are workspace-level and can be organized into groups with a parent-child hierarchy. Only child labels (not group labels) can be directly applied to projects.' (ProjectLabel)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectLabelCreateInput.retiredAt' description changed from 'When the label was retired.' to 'The time at which the label was retired. Set to null to restore a retired label.' (ProjectLabelCreateInput.retiredAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectLabelCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new project label. A name is required. The label is created as a workspace-level label available to all projects.' (ProjectLabelCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectLabelPayload' has description 'The result of a project label mutation.' (ProjectLabelPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectLabelUpdateInput.retiredAt' description changed from 'When the label was retired.' to 'The time at which the label was retired. Set to null to restore a retired label.' (ProjectLabelUpdateInput.retiredAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectLabelUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing project label. All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.' (ProjectLabelUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectMilestone.currentProgress' description changed from '[Internal] The current progress of the project milestone.' to '[Internal] The current progress of the milestone, broken down by issue status category.' (ProjectMilestone.currentProgress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectMilestone.documentContent' description changed from 'The content of the project milestone description.' to 'The rich-text content of the milestone description. Null if no description has been set.' (ProjectMilestone.documentContent)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectMilestone.progressHistory' description changed from '[Internal] The progress history of the project milestone.' to '[Internal] The progress history of the milestone, tracking issue completion over time.' (ProjectMilestone.progressHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectMilestone.project' description changed from 'The project of the milestone.' to 'The project that this milestone belongs to.' (ProjectMilestone.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectMilestone.targetDate' description changed from 'The planned completion date of the milestone.' to 'The planned completion date of the milestone. Null if no target date is set.' (ProjectMilestone.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A milestone for a project.' on type 'ProjectMilestone' has changed to 'A milestone within a project. Milestones break a project into phases or target checkpoints, each with its own target date and set of issues. Issues can be assigned to a milestone to track progress toward that checkpoint.' (ProjectMilestone)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectMilestoneCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new project milestone.' (ProjectMilestoneCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectMilestoneMoveInput' has description '[Internal] Input for moving a project milestone to another project.' (ProjectMilestoneMoveInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectMilestoneMovePayload' has description '[Internal] The result of a project milestone move mutation.' (ProjectMilestoneMovePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectMilestonePayload' has description 'The result of a project milestone mutation.' (ProjectMilestonePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectMilestoneUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing project milestone.' (ProjectMilestoneUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.actor' description changed from 'The user that caused the notification.' to 'The user that caused the notification. Null if the notification was triggered by a non-user actor such as an integration, external user, or system event.' (ProjectNotification.actor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.emailedAt' description changed from 'The time at when an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null, if no email
  reminder has been sent.' to 'The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.' (ProjectNotification.emailedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.externalUserActor' description changed from 'The external user that caused the notification.' to 'The external user that caused the notification. Populated when the notification was triggered by an external user (e.g., a commenter from a connected integration like Slack or GitHub) rather than a Linear workspace member.' (ProjectNotification.externalUserActor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.readAt' description changed from 'The time at when the user marked the notification as read. Null, if the the user hasn't read the notification' to 'The time at which the user marked the notification as read. Null if the notification is unread.' (ProjectNotification.readAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.snoozedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until a notification will be snoozed. After that it will appear in the inbox again.' to 'The time until which a notification is snoozed. After this time, the notification reappears in the user's inbox. Null if the notification is not currently snoozed.' (ProjectNotification.snoozedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.type' description changed from 'Notification type.' to 'Notification type. Determines the kind of event that triggered this notification and which associated entity fields will be populated.' (ProjectNotification.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.unsnoozedAt' description changed from 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed..' to 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed. Null if the notification has not been unsnoozed.' (ProjectNotification.unsnoozedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotification.user' description changed from 'The user that received the notification.' to 'The recipient user of this notification.' (ProjectNotification.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A project related notification.' on type 'ProjectNotification' has changed to 'A notification related to a project, such as being added as a member or lead, project updates, comments, or mentions on the project or its milestones.' (ProjectNotification)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.active' description changed from 'Whether the subscription is active or not.' to 'Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.active)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.contextViewType' description changed from 'The type of view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a specific view type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.contextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.customView' description changed from 'The contextual custom view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The custom view that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.customView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.customer' description changed from 'The customer associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The customer that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.customer)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.cycle' description changed from 'The contextual cycle view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The cycle that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.cycle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.initiative' description changed from 'The contextual initiative view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The initiative that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.label' description changed from 'The contextual label view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.label)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes' description changed from 'The type of subscription.' to 'The notification event types that this subscription will deliver to the subscriber.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.subscriber' description changed from 'The user that subscribed to receive notifications.' to 'The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.subscriber)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.team' description changed from 'The team associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The team that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.user' description changed from 'The user view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The user that this notification subscription is scoped to, for user-specific view subscriptions. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType' description changed from 'The type of user view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A project notification subscription.' on type 'ProjectNotificationSubscription' has changed to 'A notification subscription scoped to a specific project. The subscriber receives notifications for events related to this project, such as updates, comments, and membership changes.' (ProjectNotificationSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectPayload' has description 'The result of a project mutation.' (ProjectPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.anchorType' description changed from 'The type of anchor on the project end of the relation.' to 'The type of anchor on the source project end of the relation, indicating whether it is anchored to the project itself or a specific milestone.' (ProjectRelation.anchorType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.project' description changed from 'The project whose relationship is being described.' to 'The source project in the dependency relation.' (ProjectRelation.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.projectMilestone' description changed from 'The milestone within the project whose relationship is being described.' to 'The specific milestone within the source project that the relation is anchored to. Null if the relation applies to the project as a whole.' (ProjectRelation.projectMilestone)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.relatedAnchorType' description changed from 'The type of anchor on the relatedProject end of the relation.' to 'The type of anchor on the target project end of the relation, indicating whether it is anchored to the project itself or a specific milestone.'   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.relatedProject' description changed from 'The related project.' to 'The target project in the dependency relation.' (ProjectRelation.relatedProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.relatedProjectMilestone' description changed from 'The milestone within the related project whose relationship is being described.' to 'The specific milestone within the target project that the relation is anchored to.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.type' description changed from 'The relationship of the project with the related project.' to 'The type of dependency relationship from the project to the related project (e.g., blocks).' (ProjectRelation.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectRelation.user' description changed from 'The last user who created or modified the relation.' to 'The user who last created or modified the relation. Null if the user has been deleted.' (ProjectRelation.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectSearchResult.status' description changed from 'The status that the project is associated with.' to 'The current project status. Defines the project's position in its lifecycle (e.g., backlog, planned, started, paused, completed, canceled).' (ProjectSearchResult.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectSearchResult.targetDate' description changed from 'The estimated completion date of the project.' to 'The estimated completion date of the project. Null if no target date is set.' (ProjectSearchResult.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectSearchResult.targetDateResolution' description changed from 'The resolution of the project's estimated completion date.' to 'The resolution of the project's estimated completion date, indicating whether it refers to a specific month, quarter, half-year, or year.' (ProjectSearchResult.targetDateResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectStatus.color' description changed from 'The UI color of the status as a HEX string.' to 'The color of the status as a HEX string, used for display in the UI.' (ProjectStatus.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectStatus.indefinite' description changed from 'Whether or not a project can be in this status indefinitely.' to 'Whether a project can remain in this status indefinitely. When false, projects in this status may trigger reminders or auto-archiving after a period of inactivity.' (ProjectStatus.indefinite)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectStatus.position' description changed from 'The position of the status in the workspace's project flow.' to 'The position of the status within its type group in the workspace's project flow. Used for ordering statuses of the same type.' (ProjectStatus.position)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectStatus.type' description changed from 'The type of the project status.' to 'The category type of the project status (e.g., backlog, planned, started, paused, completed, canceled). Determines the status's behavior and position in the project lifecycle.' (ProjectStatus.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A project status.' on type 'ProjectStatus' has changed to 'A custom project status within a workspace. Statuses are grouped by type (backlog, planned, started, paused, completed, canceled) and define the lifecycle stages a project can move through. Each workspace can customize the names and colors of its project statuses.' (ProjectStatus)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectStatusCountPayload' has description 'The count of projects using a specific project status.' (ProjectStatusCountPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectStatusCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new project status.' (ProjectStatusCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectStatusPayload' has description 'The result of a project status mutation.' (ProjectStatusPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectStatusUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing project status.' (ProjectStatusUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectUpdate.health' description changed from 'The health of the project at the time of the update.' to 'The health of the project at the time this update was posted. Possible values are onTrack, atRisk, or offTrack.' (ProjectUpdate.health)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectUpdate.infoSnapshot' description changed from '[Internal] Serialized JSON representing current state of the project properties when posting the project update.' to '[Internal] A snapshot of project properties at the time the update was posted, including team, milestone, and issue statistics. Used to compute diffs between consecutive updates.' (ProjectUpdate.infoSnapshot)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectUpdate.isDiffHidden' description changed from 'Whether project update diff should be hidden.' to 'Whether the diff between this update and the previous one should be hidden in the UI.' (ProjectUpdate.isDiffHidden)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ProjectUpdate.project' description changed from 'The project that the update is associated with.' to 'The project that this status update was posted to.' (ProjectUpdate.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An update associated with a project.' on type 'ProjectUpdate' has changed to 'A status update posted to a project. Project updates communicate progress, health, and blockers to stakeholders. Each update captures the project's health at the time of writing and includes a rich-text body with the update content.' (ProjectUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectUpdateCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new project update.' (ProjectUpdateCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.canceledAt' description changed from 'The date when the project was canceled.' to 'The time at which the project was canceled.' (ProjectUpdateInput.canceledAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.completedAt' description changed from 'The date when the project was completed.' to 'The time at which the project was completed.' (ProjectUpdateInput.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.frequencyResolution' description changed from 'The frequency resolution.' to 'The resolution type for the update reminder frequency (e.g., weekly, biweekly).' (ProjectUpdateInput.frequencyResolution)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.projectUpdateRemindersPausedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until which project update reminders are paused.' to 'The time until which project update reminders are paused. Set to null to resume reminders.' (ProjectUpdateInput.projectUpdateRemindersPausedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.trashed' description changed from 'Whether the project has been trashed.' to 'Whether the project has been trashed. Set to true to trash, or null to restore.' (ProjectUpdateInput.trashed)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequency' description changed from 'The frequency at which to prompt for updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace.' to 'The frequency at which to prompt for project updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace settings.' (ProjectUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequency)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequencyInWeeks' description changed from 'The n-weekly frequency at which to prompt for updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace.' to 'The n-weekly frequency at which to prompt for project updates. When not set, reminders are inherited from workspace settings.' (ProjectUpdateInput.updateReminderFrequencyInWeeks)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.updateRemindersDay' description changed from 'The day at which to prompt for updates.' to 'The day of the week on which to prompt for project updates.' (ProjectUpdateInput.updateRemindersDay)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ProjectUpdateInput.updateRemindersHour' description changed from 'The hour at which to prompt for updates.' to 'The hour of the day (0-23) at which to prompt for project updates.' (ProjectUpdateInput.updateRemindersHour)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing project. All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated. Setting a field to null (where supported) will clear the value.' (ProjectUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectUpdatePayload' has description 'The result of a project update mutation.' (ProjectUpdatePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectUpdateReminderPayload' has description 'The result of a project update reminder mutation.' (ProjectUpdateReminderPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ProjectUpdateUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing project update.' (ProjectUpdateUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.baseSha' description changed from 'The base SHA of the pull request.' to 'The Git SHA of the base commit on the target branch that the pull request is compared against. Null if not yet synced.' (PullRequest.baseSha)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.checks' description changed from '[Internal] The checks associated with the pull request.' to '[Internal] The CI/CD checks and status checks associated with the pull request, synced from the hosting provider.' (PullRequest.checks)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.commits' description changed from '[ALPHA] The commits associated with the pull request.' to '[ALPHA] The commits included in the pull request, synced from the hosting provider. Includes metadata such as SHA, message, diff stats, and author information.' (PullRequest.commits)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.creator' description changed from '[Internal] The user who created the pull request.' to '[Internal] The Linear user who created the pull request. Null if the creator is an external user not mapped to a Linear account, or if the creator's account has been deleted.' (PullRequest.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.headSha' description changed from 'The head SHA of the pull request.' to 'The Git SHA of the latest commit on the source branch. Updated as new commits are pushed. Null if not yet synced.' (PullRequest.headSha)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.mergeCommit' description changed from 'The merge commit created when the PR was merged.' to 'The merge commit created when the pull request was merged. Null if the pull request has not been merged or if the merge commit data is not available.' (PullRequest.mergeCommit)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.mergeSettings' description changed from 'Merge settings for this pull request.' to 'Merge settings and allowed merge methods for this pull request's repository. Null if the settings have not been synced from the provider.' (PullRequest.mergeSettings)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.number' description changed from 'The number of the pull request in the version control system.' to 'The pull request number as assigned by the hosting provider (e.g., #123 on GitHub). Unique within a repository.' (PullRequest.number)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.slugId' description changed from 'The pull request's unique URL slug.' to 'The pull request's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs within the Linear app.' (PullRequest.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.sourceBranch' description changed from 'The source branch of the pull request.' to 'The source (head) branch of the pull request that contains the proposed changes.' (PullRequest.sourceBranch)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.status' description changed from 'The status of the pull request.' to 'The current status of the pull request (open, closed, merged, or draft). Synced from the hosting provider.' (PullRequest.status)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.targetBranch' description changed from 'The target branch of the pull request.' to 'The target (base) branch that the pull request will be merged into.' (PullRequest.targetBranch)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequest.url' description changed from 'The URL of the pull request in the version control system.' to 'The URL of the pull request on the hosting provider (e.g., a GitHub or GitLab URL).' (PullRequest.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A pull request in a version control system.' on type 'PullRequest' has changed to '[Internal] A pull or merge request from a connected version control system (GitHub or GitLab). Pull requests are automatically linked to Linear issues via branch names or commit messages containing issue identifiers. They track the full lifecycle including status, reviewers, checks, and merge state, and are synced bidirectionally with the hosting provider.' (PullRequest)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCheck.completedAt' description changed from 'The date/time at which when the check was completed.' to 'The date and time when the check finished running. Null if the check has not completed yet.' (PullRequestCheck.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCheck.isRequired' description changed from 'Whether the check is required.' to 'Whether this check is required to pass before the pull request can be merged. Null if the required status is unknown.' (PullRequestCheck.isRequired)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCheck.startedAt' description changed from 'The date/time at which when the check was started.' to 'The date and time when the check started running. Null if the check has not started yet.' (PullRequestCheck.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCheck.url' description changed from 'The URL of the check.' to 'The URL to view the check details on the hosting provider. Null if no URL is available.' (PullRequestCheck.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCheck.workflowName' description changed from 'The name of the workflow that triggered the check.' to 'The name of the CI workflow that triggered the check (e.g., 'CI' or 'Build and Test'). Null if the check is not part of a named workflow.' (PullRequestCheck.workflowName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[ALPHA] A pull request check.' on type 'PullRequestCheck' has changed to '[ALPHA] A CI/CD check or status check associated with a pull request (e.g., a GitHub Actions workflow run or a required status check).' (PullRequestCheck)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCommit.changedFiles' description changed from 'The number of changed files if available.' to 'The number of files changed in this commit. Null if the hosting provider did not include this information.' (PullRequestCommit.changedFiles)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCommit.committedAt' description changed from 'The timestamp when the commit was committed (ISO 8601 string).' to 'The timestamp when the commit was committed, as an ISO 8601 string.' (PullRequestCommit.committedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestCommit.isMergeCommit' description changed from 'Whether this commit has multiple parents.' to 'Whether this commit is a merge commit (has multiple parents). Merge commits are typically filtered out when counting diff stats.' (PullRequestCommit.isMergeCommit)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[ALPHA] A pull request commit.' on type 'PullRequestCommit' has changed to '[ALPHA] A Git commit associated with a pull request, including metadata about the commit's content and authors.' (PullRequestCommit)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestMergeSettings.isMergeQueueEnabled' description changed from 'Whether merge queue is enabled for this repository.' to 'Whether a merge queue is enabled for pull requests in this repository. When enabled, merges go through an ordered queue rather than being merged directly.' (PullRequestMergeSettings.isMergeQueueEnabled)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestMergeSettings.mergeQueueMergeMethod' description changed from 'The method used to merge a pull request.' to 'The merge method used by the merge queue, if applicable. Null when merge queue is not enabled or the method is not specified.' (PullRequestMergeSettings.mergeQueueMergeMethod)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] Merge settings for a pull request' on type 'PullRequestMergeSettings' has changed to '[Internal] Merge settings and capabilities for a pull request's repository, including which merge methods are allowed and whether features like merge queues and auto-merge are enabled.' (PullRequestMergeSettings)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.actor' description changed from 'The user that caused the notification.' to 'The user that caused the notification. Null if the notification was triggered by a non-user actor such as an integration, external user, or system event.' (PullRequestNotification.actor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.emailedAt' description changed from 'The time at when an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null, if no email
  reminder has been sent.' to 'The time at which an email reminder for this notification was sent to the user. Null if no email reminder has been sent.' (PullRequestNotification.emailedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.externalUserActor' description changed from 'The external user that caused the notification.' to 'The external user that caused the notification. Populated when the notification was triggered by an external user (e.g., a commenter from a connected integration like Slack or GitHub) rather than a Linear workspace member.' (PullRequestNotification.externalUserActor)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.readAt' description changed from 'The time at when the user marked the notification as read. Null, if the the user hasn't read the notification' to 'The time at which the user marked the notification as read. Null if the notification is unread.' (PullRequestNotification.readAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.snoozedUntilAt' description changed from 'The time until a notification will be snoozed. After that it will appear in the inbox again.' to 'The time until which a notification is snoozed. After this time, the notification reappears in the user's inbox. Null if the notification is not currently snoozed.' (PullRequestNotification.snoozedUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.type' description changed from 'Notification type.' to 'Notification type. Determines the kind of event that triggered this notification and which associated entity fields will be populated.' (PullRequestNotification.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.unsnoozedAt' description changed from 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed..' to 'The time at which a notification was unsnoozed. Null if the notification has not been unsnoozed.' (PullRequestNotification.unsnoozedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PullRequestNotification.user' description changed from 'The user that received the notification.' to 'The recipient user of this notification.' (PullRequestNotification.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A pull request related notification.' on type 'PullRequestNotification' has changed to 'A notification related to a pull request, such as review requests, approvals, comments, check failures, or merge queue events.' (PullRequestNotification)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Input for referencing a pull request by repository and number.' on type 'PullRequestReferenceInput' has changed to 'A reference to a pull request by its repository owner, name, and pull request number. Used during release sync to look up pull requests and associate their linked issues with the release.' (PullRequestReferenceInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A user's web or mobile push notification subscription.' on type 'PushSubscription' has changed to 'A device registration for receiving push notifications. Each PushSubscription represents a specific browser or mobile device endpoint where a user has opted in to receive real-time push notifications. A user may have multiple push subscriptions across different devices and browsers.' (PushSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'PushSubscriptionCreateInput.data' description changed from 'The data of the subscription in stringified JSON format.' to 'The push subscription data in stringified JSON format. For web subscriptions, this must contain keys, endpoint, and expirationTime fields per the Web Push API specification. For mobile subscriptions, this contains the device token.' (PushSubscriptionCreateInput.data)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'PushSubscriptionCreateInput.type' description changed from 'Whether this is a subscription payload for Google Cloud Messaging or Apple Push Notification service.' to 'The type of push subscription: 'web' for browser-based Web Push API, 'apple' for Apple Push Notification service (or 'appleDevelopment' for sandbox), or 'firebase' for Firebase Cloud Messaging (Android).' (PushSubscriptionCreateInput.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'PushSubscriptionCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a push subscription to receive push notifications on a device or browser.' (PushSubscriptionCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'PushSubscriptionPayload.entity' description changed from 'The push subscription that was created or updated.' to 'The push subscription that was created or deleted.' (PushSubscriptionPayload.entity)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'PushSubscriptionPayload' has description 'Return type for push subscription mutations.' (PushSubscriptionPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'PushSubscriptionTestPayload' has description 'Return type for the push subscription test query.' (PushSubscriptionTestPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.administrableTeams' description changed from 'All teams you the user can administrate. (Query.administrableTeams)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.customViews' description changed from 'Custom views for the user.' to 'All custom views accessible to the user, including personal views and shared workspace views. Excludes views scoped to a specific project or initiative.' (Query.customViews)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Query.customerNeeds' changed from 'Filter returned customers needs.' to 'Filter returned customer needs.' (Query.customerNeeds.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.customerStatus' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier of the customer status to retrieve.' (Query.customerStatus.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.customerTier' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier of the customer tier to retrieve.' (Query.customerTier.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.customerTiers' description changed from 'All customer tiers.' to 'All customer tiers defined in the workspace.' (Query.customerTiers)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.customers' description changed from 'All customers.' to 'All customers in the workspace, with optional filtering and sorting.' (Query.customers)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'sorts' on field 'Query.customers' changed from 'Sort returned customers.' to 'Sort criteria for the returned customers. Up to 3 sort fields can be specified.' (Query.customers.sorts)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.cycle' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier of the cycle to retrieve.' (Query.cycle.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Query.cycles' changed from 'Filter returned users.' to 'Filter returned cycles.' (Query.cycles.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.document' description changed from 'One specific document.' to 'A specific document by ID or slug.' (Query.document)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.document' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier or slug of the document to retrieve.' (Query.document.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.documentContentHistory' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier of the document content to retrieve history for.' (Query.documentContentHistory.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'entryIds' on field 'Query.documentContentHistoryEntries' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifiers of the history entries to retrieve. Maximum 10 per request.' (Query.documentContentHistoryEntries.entryIds)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.documents' description changed from 'All documents in the workspace.' to 'All documents the user has access to in the workspace.' (Query.documents)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.emoji' description changed from 'A specific emoji.' to 'A specific custom emoji by ID or name.' (Query.emoji)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.emojis' description changed from 'All custom emojis.' to 'All custom emojis in the workspace.' (Query.emojis)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.entityExternalLink' changed from 'undefined' to 'The identifier of the entity external link to retrieve.' (Query.entityExternalLink.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.externalUser' description changed from 'One specific external user.' to 'Retrieves a single external user by their identifier.' (Query.externalUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.externalUsers' description changed from 'All external users for the organization.' to 'All external users for the organization. External users are people who interact with Linear through integrated services (Slack, Jira, GitHub, etc.) without having a Linear account.' (Query.externalUsers)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.favorite' description changed from 'One specific favorite.' to 'A specific favorite by ID.' (Query.favorite)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.favorites' description changed from 'The user's favorites.' to 'The authenticated user's favorites. Returns all bookmarked items that appear in the user's sidebar.' (Query.favorites)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiative' description changed from 'One specific initiative.' to 'Returns a single initiative by its identifier or URL slug.' (Query.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiativeRelation' description changed from 'One specific initiative relation.' to 'Returns a single initiative relation by its identifier.' (Query.initiativeRelation)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiativeRelations' description changed from 'All initiative relationships.' to 'Returns all initiative parent-child relations in the workspace.' (Query.initiativeRelations)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiativeToProject' description changed from 'One specific initiativeToProject.' to 'Returns a single initiative-to-project association by its identifier.' (Query.initiativeToProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiativeToProjects' description changed from 'returns a list of initiative to project entities.' to 'Returns all initiative-to-project associations in the workspace.' (Query.initiativeToProjects)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Query.initiativeUpdate' description changed from 'A specific initiative update.' to 'Returns a single initiative update by its identifier.' (Query.initiativeUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'id' on field 'Query.initiativeUpdate' changed from 'The identifier of the initiative update to retrieve.' to 'The identifier of the initiative update to retrieve.' (Query.initiativeUpdate.id)

(Query.semanticSearch)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'RateLimitPayload.identifier' description changed from 'The identifier we rate limit on.' to 'The identifier being rate limited, typically the API key or user ID.' (RateLimitPayload.identifier)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'RateLimitResultPayload.period' description changed from 'The period in which the rate limit is fully replenished in ms.' to 'The duration in milliseconds of the rate limit window.
