  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'RateLimitResultPayload.reset' description changed from 'The timestamp after the rate limit is fully replenished as a UNIX timestamp.' to 'The UNIX timestamp (in milliseconds) at which the rate limit will be fully replenished.' (RateLimitResultPayload.reset)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'RateLimitResultPayload.type' description changed from 'What is being rate limited.' to 'The specific type of rate limit being tracked, such as query complexity or mutation count.' (RateLimitResultPayload.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RateLimitResultPayload' has description 'The state of a specific rate limit type, including remaining quota and reset timing.' (RateLimitResultPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.comment' description changed from 'The comment that the reaction is associated with.' to 'The comment that the reaction is associated with. Null if the reaction belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Reaction.comment)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.emoji' description changed from 'Name of the reaction's emoji.' to 'The name of the emoji used for this reaction. For custom workspace emojis, this is the custom emoji name; for standard emojis, this is the normalized emoji name.' (Reaction.emoji)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.externalUser' description changed from 'The external user that created the reaction.' to 'The external user that created the reaction through an integration. Null if the reaction was created by a workspace user.' (Reaction.externalUser)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.initiativeUpdate' description changed from 'The initiative update that the reaction is associated with.' to 'The initiative update that the reaction is associated with. Null if the reaction belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Reaction.initiativeUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.issue' description changed from 'The issue that the reaction is associated with.' to 'The issue that the reaction is associated with. Null if the reaction belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Reaction.issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.post' description changed from 'The post that the reaction is associated with.' to 'The post that the reaction is associated with. Null if the reaction belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Reaction.post)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.projectUpdate' description changed from 'The project update that the reaction is associated with.' to 'The project update that the reaction is associated with. Null if the reaction belongs to a different parent entity type.' (Reaction.projectUpdate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Reaction.user' description changed from 'The user that created the reaction.' to 'The workspace user that created the reaction. Null if the reaction was created by an external user through an integration.' (Reaction.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A reaction associated with a comment or a project update.' on type 'Reaction' has changed to 'An emoji reaction on a comment, issue, project update, initiative update, post, pull request, or pull request comment. Each reaction is associated with exactly one parent entity and is created by either a workspace user or an external user. Reactions are persisted individually but surfaced on their parent entities as aggregated reactionData.' (Reaction)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReactionCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new reaction.' (ReactionCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReactionPayload.reaction' has description 'The reaction that was created.' (ReactionPayload.reaction)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReactionPayload.success' has description 'Whether the operation was successful.' (ReactionPayload.success)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReactionPayload' has description 'The result of a reaction mutation.' (ReactionPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'releaseNotes' was added to object type 'Release' (Release.releaseNotes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.canceledAt' description changed from 'The time at which the release was canceled.' to 'The time at which the release was canceled. Set automatically when the release moves to a canceled stage. Reset to null if the release moves back to a non-canceled stage.' (Release.canceledAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.commitSha' description changed from 'The commit SHA associated with this release.' to 'The Git commit SHA associated with this release. Used for SHA-based idempotency when completing releases and for linking releases to specific points in the repository history. Null if the release was created without a commit reference.' (Release.commitSha)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.completedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the release was completed.' to 'The time at which the release was completed. Set automatically when the release moves to a completed stage. Reset to null if the release moves back to a non-completed stage.' (Release.completedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.creator' description changed from 'The user who created the release.' to 'The user who created the release. Null if the release was created by a non-user context such as an access key or automation.' (Release.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.currentProgress' description changed from '[Internal] The current progress of the release.' to '[Internal] The current progress summary for the release, including counts of issues by workflow state type (e.g., completed, in progress, unstarted).' (Release.currentProgress)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.description' description changed from 'The release's description.' to 'The description of the release in plain text or markdown. Null if no description has been set.' (Release.description)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.pipeline' description changed from 'The pipeline this release belongs to.' to 'The release pipeline that this release belongs to. A release always belongs to exactly one pipeline.' (Release.pipeline)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.progressHistory' description changed from '[Internal] The progress history of the release.' to '[Internal] The historical progress snapshots for the release, tracking how issue completion has evolved over time.' (Release.progressHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.slugId' description changed from 'The release's unique URL slug.' to 'The release's unique URL slug, used to construct human-readable URLs for the release.' (Release.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.stage' description changed from 'The current stage of the release.' to 'The current stage of the release within its pipeline (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Changing the stage triggers lifecycle timestamp updates and may move non-closed issues to a new release when completing a scheduled pipeline release.' (Release.stage)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.startDate' description changed from 'The estimated start date of the release.' to 'The estimated start date of the release. This is a date-only value without a time component. Automatically set to today when the release moves to a started stage if not already set. Null if no start date has been specified.' (Release.startDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.startedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the release was started.' to 'The time at which the release first entered a started stage. Null if the release has not yet been started.' (Release.startedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.targetDate' description changed from 'The estimated completion date of the release.' to 'The estimated completion date of the release. This is a date-only value without a time component. Null if no target date has been specified.' (Release.targetDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.trashed' description changed from 'A flag that indicates whether the release is in the trash bin.' to 'A flag that indicates whether the release is in the trash bin. Trashed releases are archived and will be permanently deleted after a retention period. Null when the release is not trashed.' (Release.trashed)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.url' description changed from 'Release URL.' to 'The URL to the release page in the Linear app.' (Release.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Release.version' description changed from 'The version of the release.' to 'The version identifier for this release (e.g., 'v1.2.3' or a short commit hash). Must be unique within the pipeline. Null if no version has been assigned.' (Release.version)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release.' on type 'Release' has changed to '[Internal] A release that bundles issues together for a software deployment or version. Releases belong to a release pipeline and progress through stages (e.g., planned, started, completed, canceled). Issues are associated with releases via the IssueToRelease join entity, and the release tracks lifecycle timestamps such as when it was started, completed, or canceled.' (Release)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseCompleteInput' has description 'Input for completing a release in a specific pipeline.' (ReleaseCompleteInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseCompleteInputBase' has description 'Base input for completing a release. Contains the optional version and commit SHA. The pipeline ID is provided separately or inferred from the access key.' (ReleaseCompleteInputBase)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Debug sink for release creation diagnostics.' on type 'ReleaseDebugSinkInput' has changed to 'Diagnostic data captured during release sync, including inspected commits, discovered issue references, and pull request metadata. Stored on the release for debugging release association issues.' (ReleaseDebugSinkInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseHistory.release' description changed from 'The release that the history is associated with.' to 'The release that this history record tracks changes for.' (ReleaseHistory.release)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release history containing relevant change events.' on type 'ReleaseHistory' has changed to '[Internal] A release history record containing a batch of chronologically ordered change events for a release. Each record holds up to 30 entries, and new records are created once the current record is full and a time window has elapsed. Tracks changes to name, description, version, stage, dates, pipeline, and archive status.' (ReleaseHistory)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleasePayload' has description 'The result of a release mutation, containing the release that was created or updated and a success indicator.' (ReleasePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.approximateReleaseCount' description changed from '[ALPHA] The number of non-archived releases in this pipeline.' to '[ALPHA] The approximate number of non-archived releases in this pipeline. This is a denormalized count that is updated when releases are created or archived, and may not reflect the exact count at all times.' (ReleasePipeline.approximateReleaseCount)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.includePathPatterns' description changed from 'Glob patterns to include commits affecting matching file paths.' to 'Glob patterns to filter commits by file path. When non-empty, only commits that modify files matching at least one pattern will be included in release syncs. An empty array means all commits are included regardless of file paths.' (ReleasePipeline.includePathPatterns)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.isProduction' description changed from '[ALPHA] Whether this pipeline targets a production environment. Default to true.' to '[ALPHA] Whether this pipeline targets a production environment. Defaults to true. Used to distinguish production pipelines from staging or development pipelines.' (ReleasePipeline.isProduction)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.slugId' description changed from 'The pipeline's unique slug identifier.' to 'The pipeline's unique slug identifier, used in URLs and for lookup by human-readable identifier instead of UUID.' (ReleasePipeline.slugId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.type' description changed from 'The type of the pipeline.' to 'The type of the pipeline, which determines how releases are created and managed. Continuous pipelines create a completed release per sync, while scheduled pipelines accumulate issues in a started release.' (ReleasePipeline.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleasePipeline.url' description changed from '[Internal] Release pipeline URL.' to '[Internal] The URL to the release pipeline's releases list in the Linear app.' (ReleasePipeline.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release pipeline.' on type 'ReleasePipeline' has changed to '[Internal] A release pipeline that defines a release workflow with ordered stages. Pipelines can be continuous (each sync creates a completed release) or scheduled (issues accumulate in a started release that is explicitly completed). Pipelines are associated with teams and can filter commits by file path patterns.' (ReleasePipeline)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleasePipelineCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new release pipeline.' (ReleasePipelineCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleasePipelinePayload' has description 'The result of a release pipeline mutation.' (ReleasePipelinePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A type of release pipeline.' on type 'ReleasePipelineType' has changed to 'The type of a release pipeline, which determines how releases are created and managed. Continuous pipelines create a new completed release for each sync. Scheduled pipelines accumulate issues into a started release that is explicitly completed.' (ReleasePipelineType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleasePipelineUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing release pipeline.' (ReleasePipelineUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseStage.color' description changed from 'The UI color of the stage as a HEX string.' to 'The display color of the stage as a HEX string (e.g., '#0f783c'), used for visual representation in the UI.' (ReleaseStage.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseStage.frozen' description changed from 'Whether this stage is frozen. Only applicable to started type stages.' to 'Whether this stage is frozen. Only applicable to started-type stages. When a stage is frozen, automated release syncs will not target releases in this stage, and new issues will not be automatically added. At least one started stage in the pipeline must remain non-frozen.' (ReleaseStage.frozen)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseStage.pipeline' description changed from 'The pipeline this stage belongs to.' to 'The release pipeline that this stage belongs to. A stage always belongs to exactly one pipeline.' (ReleaseStage.pipeline)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseStage.position' description changed from 'The position of the stage.' to 'The position of the stage within its pipeline, used for ordering stages in the UI. Lower values appear first.' (ReleaseStage.position)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ReleaseStage.type' description changed from 'The type of the stage.' to 'The lifecycle type of the stage (planned, started, completed, or canceled). The type determines what lifecycle timestamps are set on a release when it enters this stage.' (ReleaseStage.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Internal] A release stage.' on type 'ReleaseStage' has changed to '[Internal] A stage within a release pipeline that represents a phase in the release lifecycle (e.g., Planned, In Progress, Completed, Canceled). Releases progress through stages as they move toward production. Started-type stages can be frozen to prevent new issues from being automatically synced into releases at that stage.' (ReleaseStage)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseStageCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new release stage.' (ReleaseStageCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseStagePayload' has description 'The result of a release stage mutation.' (ReleaseStagePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A type of release stage.' on type 'ReleaseStageType' has changed to 'The type of a release stage, which determines the release's lifecycle state. Types include planned, started, completed, and canceled. Each pipeline must have at least one stage of each type, though only started stages may have multiple instances.' (ReleaseStageType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseStageUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing release stage.' (ReleaseStageUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The release data to sync.' on type 'ReleaseSyncInput' has changed to 'Input for syncing release data to a specific pipeline. Extends the base sync input with the target pipeline identifier.' (ReleaseSyncInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Base release sync data without pipeline specification.' on type 'ReleaseSyncInputBase' has changed to 'Base input for syncing release data, containing the commit SHA, issue references, pull request references, and optional metadata. Does not include the pipeline ID, which is provided separately or inferred from the access key.' (ReleaseSyncInputBase)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Input for updating a release by pipeline.' on type 'ReleaseUpdateByPipelineInput' has changed to 'Input for updating a release by pipeline identifier. Extends the base update input with the target pipeline identifier.' (ReleaseUpdateByPipelineInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseUpdateByPipelineInputBase' has description 'Base input for updating a release by pipeline. Contains optional version and stage name. The pipeline ID is provided separately or inferred from the access key.' (ReleaseUpdateByPipelineInputBase)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ReleaseUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing release.' (ReleaseUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Information about the source repository.' on type 'RepositoryDataInput' has changed to 'Metadata about the source code repository from which a release is being synced, including the hosting provider and repository coordinates.' (RepositoryDataInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RepositorySuggestion' has description 'A suggested code repository that may be relevant for implementing an issue.' (RepositorySuggestion)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RepositorySuggestionsPayload' has description 'The result of a repository suggestions query, containing the list of suggested repositories.' (RepositorySuggestionsPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Roadmap.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the roadmap.' to 'The workspace of the roadmap.' (Roadmap.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Roadmap.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the roadmap within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the roadmap within the workspace.' (Roadmap.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Deprecated] A roadmap for projects.' on type 'Roadmap' has changed to '[Deprecated] A roadmap for grouping projects. Use Initiative instead, which supersedes this entity and provides richer hierarchy and tracking capabilities.' (Roadmap)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'RoadmapCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the roadmap within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the roadmap within the workspace.' (RoadmapCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new roadmap.' (RoadmapCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapPayload' has description 'The result of a roadmap mutation.' (RoadmapPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description '[Deprecated] Join table between projects and roadmaps.' on type 'RoadmapToProject' has changed to '[Deprecated] The join entity linking a project to a roadmap. Use InitiativeToProject instead, which supersedes this entity.' (RoadmapToProject)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'RoadmapToProjectCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order for the project within its organization.' to 'The sort order for the project within its workspace.' (RoadmapToProjectCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapToProjectCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new roadmap-to-project mapping.' (RoadmapToProjectCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapToProjectPayload' has description 'The result of a roadmap-to-project mutation.' (RoadmapToProjectPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'RoadmapToProjectUpdateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order for the project within its organization.' to 'The sort order for the project within its workspace.' (RoadmapToProjectUpdateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapToProjectUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing roadmap-to-project mapping.' (RoadmapToProjectUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'RoadmapUpdateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the roadmap within the organization.' to 'The sort order of the roadmap within the workspace.' (RoadmapUpdateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'RoadmapUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing roadmap.' (RoadmapUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SemanticSearchPayload.results' has description 'The list of matching search results, ordered by relevance score.' (SemanticSearchPayload.results)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Payload returned by semantic search.' on type 'SemanticSearchPayload' has changed to 'The payload returned by the semantic search query, containing the list of matching results.' (SemanticSearchPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SemanticSearchResult.document' description changed from 'The document related to the semantic search result.' to 'The document entity, if this search result is of type Document. Null for other result types.' (SemanticSearchResult.document)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SemanticSearchResult.initiative' description changed from 'The initiative related to the semantic search result.' to 'The initiative entity, if this search result is of type Initiative. Null for other result types.' (SemanticSearchResult.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SemanticSearchResult.issue' description changed from 'The issue related to the semantic search result.' to 'The issue entity, if this search result is of type Issue. Null for other result types.' (SemanticSearchResult.issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SemanticSearchResult.project' description changed from 'The project related to the semantic search result.' to 'The project entity, if this search result is of type Project. Null for other result types.' (SemanticSearchResult.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A semantic search result reference.' on type 'SemanticSearchResult' has changed to 'A reference to an entity returned by semantic search, containing its type and ID. Resolve the specific entity using the type-specific field resolvers (issue, project, initiative, document).' (SemanticSearchResult)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SesDomainIdentity.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the SES domain identity.' to 'The workspace of the SES domain identity.' (SesDomainIdentity.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'SES domain identity used for sending emails from a custom domain.' on type 'SesDomainIdentity' has changed to 'A verified Amazon SES domain identity that enables sending emails from a custom domain. Organizations configure SES domain identities to send issue notification replies and Asks auto-replies from their own domain instead of the default Linear domain. Full verification requires DKIM signing, a custom MAIL FROM domain, and organization ownership confirmation. Multiple organizations sharing the same domain each have their own SesDomainIdentity record but share the underlying SES identity.' (SesDomainIdentity)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Tuple for mapping Slack channel IDs to names.' on type 'SlackAsksTeamSettings' has changed to 'Configuration for a Linear team within a Slack Asks channel mapping. Controls whether the default Asks template is enabled for the team in a given Slack channel.' (SlackAsksTeamSettings)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Object for mapping Slack channel IDs to names and other settings.' on type 'SlackChannelNameMapping' has changed to 'Configuration for a Slack channel connected to Linear via Slack Asks. Maps the Slack channel ID and name to team assignments and auto-creation settings that control how issues are created from Slack messages in this channel.' (SlackChannelNameMapping)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'SlackPostSettingsInput.channel' has description 'The name of the Slack channel.' (SlackPostSettingsInput.channel)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'SlackPostSettingsInput.channelId' has description 'The Slack channel ID.' (SlackPostSettingsInput.channelId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'SlackPostSettingsInput.channelType' has description 'The type of the Slack channel (e.g., public, private, or DM).' (SlackPostSettingsInput.channelType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'SlackPostSettingsInput.configurationUrl' has description 'The URL to the Slack integration configuration page.' (SlackPostSettingsInput.configurationUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'SlackPostSettingsInput.teamId' description changed from 'Slack workspace id' to 'The Slack workspace ID.' (SlackPostSettingsInput.teamId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Subscription.aiPromptProgressCreated' changed from 'undefined' to '[Internal] Optional filter for AI prompt progress subscription events.' (Subscription.aiPromptProgressCreated.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Subscription.aiPromptProgressUpdated' changed from 'undefined' to '[Internal] Optional filter for AI prompt progress subscription events.' (Subscription.aiPromptProgressUpdated.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Subscription.issueCreated' changed from 'undefined' to 'Optional filter for issue subscription events.' (Subscription.issueCreated.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description for argument 'filter' on field 'Subscription.issueUpdated' changed from 'undefined' to 'Optional filter for issue subscription events.' (Subscription.issueUpdated.filter)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Summary.content' description changed from 'The summary content as a Prosemirror document.' to 'The summary content as a ProseMirror document containing the AI-generated summary text.' (Summary.content)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Summary.evalLogId' description changed from 'The evaluation log id for this summary generation.' to 'The evaluation log ID for this summary generation, used for tracking and debugging AI output quality. Null if not available.' (Summary.evalLogId)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Summary.generatedAt' description changed from 'The time at which the summary was generated.' to 'The time at which the summary content was generated or last regenerated.' (Summary.generatedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Summary.generationStatus' description changed from 'The generation status of the summary.' to 'The current generation status of the summary, indicating whether generation is in progress, completed, or failed.' (Summary.generationStatus)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Summary.issue' description changed from 'The issue this summary belongs to.' to 'The issue that this summary was generated for.' (Summary.issue)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An AI-generated summary.' on type 'Summary' has changed to 'An AI-generated summary of an issue. Each issue can have at most one summary. The summary content is stored as ProseMirror data and tracks its generation status and timing.' (Summary)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.displayName' description changed from 'The display name of the thread.' to 'A human-readable display name for the thread, derived from the external source. For Slack threads this is the channel name, for Jira it's the issue key, for email it's the sender name and count of other participants.' (SyncedExternalThread.displayName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.id' has description 'The unique identifier of this synced external thread. Auto-generated if not provided.' (SyncedExternalThread.id)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.isConnected' description changed from 'Whether this thread is syncing with the external service.' to 'Whether this thread is currently syncing comments bidirectionally with the external service. False if the external entity relation has been removed or if the thread was explicitly unsynced.' (SyncedExternalThread.isConnected)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.isPersonalIntegrationConnected' description changed from 'Whether the current user has the corresponding personal integration connected for the external service.' to 'Whether the current user has a working personal integration connected for the external service. For example, whether they have connected their personal Jira, GitHub, or Slack account. A connected personal integration may still return false if it has an authentication error.' (SyncedExternalThread.isPersonalIntegrationConnected)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.isPersonalIntegrationRequired' description changed from 'Whether a connected personal integration is required to comment in this thread.' to 'Whether a connected personal integration is required to post comments in this synced thread. True for Jira and GitHub threads, where comments must be attributed to a specific user in the external system. False for Slack and other services where the workspace integration can post on behalf of users.' (SyncedExternalThread.isPersonalIntegrationRequired)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.name' description changed from 'The display name of the source.' to 'A human-readable display name for the external source (e.g., 'Slack', 'Jira', 'GitHub').' (SyncedExternalThread.name)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.subType' description changed from 'The sub type of the external source.' to 'The specific integration service for the external source (e.g., 'slack', 'jira', 'github', 'salesforce'). Null if the source type does not have a sub-type.' (SyncedExternalThread.subType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.type' description changed from 'The type of the external source.' to 'The category of the external source (e.g., 'integration' for service integrations, 'email' for email-based threads).' (SyncedExternalThread.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'SyncedExternalThread.url' description changed from 'The external url of the thread.' to 'A URL linking to the thread in the external service. For example, a Slack message permalink, a Jira issue URL, or a GitHub issue URL.' (SyncedExternalThread.url)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A comment thread that is synced with an external source.' on type 'SyncedExternalThread' has changed to 'A comment thread that is synced with an external source such as Slack, Jira, GitHub, Salesforce, or email. Provides information about the external thread's origin, its current sync status, and whether the user has the necessary personal integration connected to participate in the thread.' (SyncedExternalThread)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'releasePipelines' was added to object type 'Team' (Team.releasePipelines)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.cycleDuration' description changed from 'The duration of a cycle in weeks.' to 'The duration of each cycle in weeks.' (Team.cycleDuration)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.cycleStartDay' description changed from 'The day of the week that a new cycle starts.' to 'The day of the week that a new cycle starts (0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, ..., 6 = Saturday).' (Team.cycleStartDay)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.cyclesEnabled' description changed from 'Whether the team uses cycles.' to 'Whether the team uses cycles for sprint-style issue management.' (Team.cyclesEnabled)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.issueCount' description changed from 'Number of issues in the team.' to 'The total number of issues in the team. By default excludes archived issues; use the includeArchived argument to include them.' (Team.issueCount)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.issues' description changed from 'Issues associated with the team.' to 'Issues belonging to the team. Supports filtering and optional inclusion of sub-team issues.' (Team.issues)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.key' description changed from 'The team's unique key. The key is used in URLs.' to 'The team's unique key, used as a prefix in issue identifiers (e.g., 'ENG' in 'ENG-123') and in URLs.' (Team.key)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.members' description changed from 'Users who are members of this team.' to 'Users who are members of this team. Supports filtering and pagination.' (Team.members)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.organization' description changed from 'The organization that the team is associated with.' to 'The workspace that the team belongs to.' (Team.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.private' description changed from 'Whether the team is private or not.' to 'Whether the team is private. Private teams are only visible to their members and require an explicit invitation to join.' (Team.private)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.retiredAt' description changed from 'The time at which the team was retired. Null if the team has not been retired.' to 'The time at which the team was retired. Retired teams no longer accept new issues or members. Null if the team has not been retired.' (Team.retiredAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.scimManaged' description changed from 'Whether the team is managed by SCIM integration.' to 'Whether the team is managed by a SCIM integration. SCIM-managed teams have their membership controlled by the identity provider.' (Team.scimManaged)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.securitySettings' description changed from 'Security settings for the team.' to 'Security settings for the team, including role-based restrictions for issue sharing, label management, member management, and template management.' (Team.securitySettings)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Team.triageEnabled' description changed from 'Whether triage mode is enabled for the team or not.' to 'Whether triage mode is enabled for the team. When enabled, issues created by non-members or integrations are routed to a triage state for review before entering the normal workflow.' (Team.triageEnabled)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'An organizational unit that contains issues.' on type 'Team' has changed to 'A team is the primary organizational unit in Linear. Issues belong to teams, and each team has its own workflow states, cycles, labels, and settings. Teams can be public (visible to all workspace members) or private (visible only to team members). Teams can also have sub-teams that inherit settings from their parent.' (Team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamMembership.owner' description changed from 'Whether the user is an owner of the team.' to 'Whether the user is an owner of the team. Team owners have elevated permissions for managing team settings, members, and resources.' (TeamMembership.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamMembership.sortOrder' description changed from 'The order of the item in the users team list.' to 'The sort order of this team in the user's personal team list. Lower values appear first.' (TeamMembership.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Defines the membership of a user to a team.' on type 'TeamMembership' has changed to 'A join entity that defines a user's membership in a team. Each membership record links a user to a team and tracks whether the user is a team owner. Users can be members of multiple teams, and their memberships determine which teams' issues and resources they can access.' (TeamMembership)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TeamMembershipCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new team membership.' (TeamMembershipCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TeamMembershipPayload' has description 'Team membership operation response.' (TeamMembershipPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TeamMembershipUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing team membership.' (TeamMembershipUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.active' description changed from 'Whether the subscription is active or not.' to 'Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.active)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.contextViewType' description changed from 'The type of view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of contextual view (e.g., active issues, backlog) that further scopes a team notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a specific view type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.contextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.customView' description changed from 'The contextual custom view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The custom view that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.customView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.customer' description changed from 'The customer associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The customer that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.customer)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.cycle' description changed from 'The contextual cycle view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The cycle that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.cycle)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.initiative' description changed from 'The contextual initiative view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The initiative that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.initiative)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.label' description changed from 'The contextual label view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.label)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes' description changed from 'The type of subscription.' to 'The notification event types that this subscription will deliver to the subscriber.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.project' description changed from 'The contextual project view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The project that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.subscriber' description changed from 'The user that subscribed to receive notifications.' to 'The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.subscriber)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.user' description changed from 'The user view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The user that this notification subscription is scoped to, for user-specific view subscriptions. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.user)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TeamNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType' description changed from 'The type of user view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.' (TeamNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A team notification subscription.' on type 'TeamNotificationSubscription' has changed to 'A notification subscription scoped to a specific team. The subscriber receives notifications for events related to issues and activity in this team.' (TeamNotificationSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TeamPayload' has description 'Team operation response.' (TeamPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TeamUpdateInput.cycleEnabledStartDate' description changed from 'The date to begin cycles on.' to 'The time at which to begin cycles.' (TeamUpdateInput.cycleEnabledStartDate)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.color' description changed from 'The color of the template icon.' to 'The hex color of the template icon. Null if no custom color has been set.' (Template.color)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.creator' description changed from 'The user who created the template.' to 'The user who created the template. Null if the creator's account has been deleted.' (Template.creator)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.description' description changed from 'Template description.' to 'A description of what the template is used for.' (Template.description)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.icon' description changed from 'The icon of the template.' to 'The icon of the template, either a decorative icon type or an emoji string. Null if no icon has been set.' (Template.icon)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.inheritedFrom' description changed from 'The original template inherited from.' to 'The parent team template this template was inherited from. Null for original (non-inherited) templates.' (Template.inheritedFrom)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.lastAppliedAt' description changed from 'The date when the template was last applied.' to 'The date when the template was last applied to create or update an entity. Null if the template has never been applied.' (Template.lastAppliedAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.lastUpdatedBy' description changed from 'The user who last updated the template.' to 'The user who last updated the template. Null if the user's account has been deleted.' (Template.lastUpdatedBy)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.organization' description changed from 'The organization that the template is associated with. If null, the template is associated with a particular team.' to 'The workspace that owns this template.' (Template.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.sortOrder' description changed from 'The sort order of the template.' to 'The sort order of the template within the templates list.' (Template.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.templateData' description changed from 'Template data.' to 'The template data as a JSON-encoded string containing the pre-filled attributes for the entity type (e.g., issue fields, project configuration, or document content).' (Template.templateData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'Template.type' description changed from 'The entity type this template is for.' to 'The entity type this template is for, such as 'issue', 'project', or 'document'.' (Template.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A template object used for creating entities faster.' on type 'Template' has changed to 'A reusable template for creating issues, projects, or documents. Templates store pre-filled field values and content as JSON data. They can be scoped to a specific team or shared across the entire workspace. Team-scoped templates may be inherited from parent teams.' (Template)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TemplateCreateInput.sortOrder' description changed from 'The position of the template in the templates list.' to 'The sort position of the template in the templates list.' (TemplateCreateInput.sortOrder)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TemplateCreateInput.templateData' description changed from 'The template data as JSON encoded attributes of the type of entity, such as an issue.' to 'The template data as JSON-encoded attributes of the target entity type, such as pre-filled issue fields, project configuration, or document content.' (TemplateCreateInput.templateData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TemplateCreateInput.type' description changed from 'The template type, e.g. 'issue'.' to 'The template type, e.g. 'issue', 'project', or 'document'.' (TemplateCreateInput.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TemplateCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new template. A name, type, and template data are required. If no team is specified, the template is shared across the workspace.' (TemplateCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TemplatePayload' has description 'The result of a template mutation.' (TemplatePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TemplateUpdateInput.templateData' description changed from 'The template data as JSON encoded attributes of the type of entity, such as an issue.' to 'The template data as JSON-encoded attributes of the target entity type, such as pre-filled issue fields, project configuration, or document content.' (TemplateUpdateInput.templateData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TemplateUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing template. All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.' (TemplateUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TimeSchedule.organization' description changed from 'The organization of the schedule.' to 'The workspace of the schedule.' (TimeSchedule.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A time schedule.' on type 'TimeSchedule' has changed to 'A time-based schedule defining on-call rotations or availability windows. Schedules contain a series of time entries, each specifying a user and their active period. They can be synced from external services (such as PagerDuty or Opsgenie) via integrations, or created manually. Schedules are used by triage responsibilities to determine who should be assigned or notified when issues enter triage.' (TimeSchedule)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TimeScheduleCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new time schedule.' (TimeScheduleCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TimeScheduleEntry.endsAt' description changed from 'The end date of the schedule in ISO 8601 date-time format.' to 'The end time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.' (TimeScheduleEntry.endsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TimeScheduleEntry.startsAt' description changed from 'The start date of the schedule in ISO 8601 date-time format.' to 'The start time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.' (TimeScheduleEntry.startsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TimeScheduleEntry' has description 'A single entry in a time schedule, defining a time range and the user responsible during that period.' (TimeScheduleEntry)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TimeScheduleEntryInput.endsAt' description changed from 'The end date of the schedule in ISO 8601 date-time format.' to 'The end time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.' (TimeScheduleEntryInput.endsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TimeScheduleEntryInput.startsAt' description changed from 'The start date of the schedule in ISO 8601 date-time format.' to 'The start time of the schedule entry in ISO 8601 date-time format.' (TimeScheduleEntryInput.startsAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TimeSchedulePayload.timeSchedule' has description 'The time schedule that was created or updated.' (TimeSchedulePayload.timeSchedule)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TimeSchedulePayload' has description 'The result of a time schedule mutation.' (TimeSchedulePayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TimeScheduleUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing time schedule.' (TimeScheduleUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'TokenUserAccountAuthInput.inviteLink' description changed from 'An optional invite link for an organization.' to 'An optional invite link for a workspace.' (TokenUserAccountAuthInput.inviteLink)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A team's triage responsibility.' on type 'TriageResponsibility' has changed to 'A team's triage responsibility configuration that defines how issues entering triage are handled. Each team can have one triage responsibility, which specifies the action to take (notify or assign) and the responsible users, determined either by a manual selection of specific users or by an on-call time schedule.' (TriageResponsibility)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TriageResponsibilityCreateInput' has description 'Input for creating a new triage responsibility.' (TriageResponsibilityCreateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TriageResponsibilityManualSelection' has description 'Manual triage responsibility configuration specifying a set of users to assign triaged issues to, with optional round-robin rotation.' (TriageResponsibilityManualSelection)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'TriageResponsibilityPayload.triageResponsibility' has description 'The triage responsibility that was created or updated.' (TriageResponsibilityPayload.triageResponsibility)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TriageResponsibilityPayload' has description 'The result of a triage responsibility mutation.' (TriageResponsibilityPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'TriageResponsibilityUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating an existing triage responsibility.' (TriageResponsibilityUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UploadFile.assetUrl' description changed from 'The asset URL for the uploaded file. (assigned automatically).' to 'The permanent asset URL where the file will be accessible after upload.' (UploadFile.assetUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UploadFile.headers' has description 'HTTP headers that must be included in the PUT request to the upload URL.' (UploadFile.headers)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UploadFile.metaData' has description 'Optional metadata associated with the upload, such as the related issue or comment ID.' (UploadFile.metaData)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UploadFile.uploadUrl' description changed from 'The signed URL the for the uploaded file. (assigned automatically).' to 'The pre-signed URL to which the file should be uploaded via a PUT request.' (UploadFile.uploadUrl)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'Object representing Google Cloud upload policy, plus additional data.' on type 'UploadFile' has changed to 'Represents a file upload destination with a pre-signed upload URL, asset URL, and required request headers for uploading to cloud storage.' (UploadFile)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UploadPayload.uploadFile' description changed from 'Object describing the file to be uploaded.' to 'The upload file details including signed URL, asset URL, and required headers. Null if the upload could not be prepared.' (UploadPayload.uploadFile)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.admin' description changed from 'Whether the user is an organization administrator.' to 'Whether the user is a workspace administrator. On Free plans, all members are treated as admins.' (User.admin)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.canAccessAnyPublicTeam' description changed from 'Whether this user can access any public team in the organization.' to 'Whether this user can access any public team in the workspace. True for non-guest members and app users with public team access.' (User.canAccessAnyPublicTeam)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.description' description changed from 'A short description of the user, either its title or bio.' to 'A short description of the user, such as their title or a brief bio.' (User.description)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.disableReason' description changed from 'Reason why is the account disabled.' to 'The reason why the user account is disabled. Null if the user is active. Possible values include admin suspension, downgrade, voluntary departure, or pending invite.' (User.disableReason)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.displayName' description changed from 'The user's display (nick) name. Unique within each organization.' to 'The user's display (nick) name. Must be unique within the workspace.' (User.displayName)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.drafts' description changed from 'The user's drafts' to 'The user's saved drafts.' (User.drafts)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.isAssignable' description changed from 'Whether the user is assignable.' to 'Whether the user can be assigned to issues. Regular users are always assignable; app users are assignable only if they have the app:assignable scope.' (User.isAssignable)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.issueDrafts' description changed from 'The user's issue drafts' to 'Issue drafts that the user has created but not yet submitted.' (User.issueDrafts)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.lastSeen' description changed from 'The last time the user was seen online.' to 'The last time the user was seen online. Updated based on user activity. Null if the user has never been seen.' (User.lastSeen)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.organization' description changed from 'Organization the user belongs to.' to 'The workspace that the user belongs to.' (User.organization)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.owner' description changed from 'Whether the user is an organization owner.' to 'Whether the user is a workspace owner, which is the highest permission level.' (User.owner)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.statusEmoji' description changed from 'The emoji to represent the user current status.' to 'The emoji representing the user's current status. Null if no status is set.' (User.statusEmoji)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.statusLabel' description changed from 'The label of the user current status.' to 'The text label of the user's current status. Null if no status is set.' (User.statusLabel)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.statusUntilAt' description changed from 'A date at which the user current status should be cleared.' to 'The date and time at which the user's current status should be automatically cleared. Null if the status has no expiration.' (User.statusUntilAt)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'User.teams' description changed from 'Teams the user is part of.' to 'Teams the user is a member of. Supports filtering by team properties.' (User.teams)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A user that has access to the the resources of an organization.' on type 'User' has changed to 'A user that belongs to a workspace. Users can have different roles (admin, member, guest, or app) that determine their level of access. Users can be members of multiple teams, and can be active or deactivated. Guest users have limited access scoped to specific teams they are invited to.' (User)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'UserAdminPayload' has description 'User admin operation response.' (UserAdminPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.active' description changed from 'Whether the subscription is active or not.' to 'Whether the subscription is active. When inactive, no notifications are generated from this subscription even though it still exists.' (UserNotificationSubscription.active)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.customView' description changed from 'The contextual custom view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The custom view that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (UserNotificationSubscription.customView)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.cycle' description changed from 'The contextual cycle view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The cycle that this notification subscription is scoped to.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.initiative' description changed from 'The contextual initiative view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The initiative that this notification subscription is scoped to.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.label' description changed from 'The contextual label view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The issue label that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (UserNotificationSubscription.label)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes' description changed from 'The type of subscription.' to 'The notification event types that this subscription will deliver to the subscriber.' (UserNotificationSubscription.notificationSubscriptionTypes)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.project' description changed from 'The contextual project view associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The project that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (UserNotificationSubscription.project)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.subscriber' description changed from 'The user that subscribed to receive notifications.' to 'The user who will receive notifications from this subscription.' (UserNotificationSubscription.subscriber)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.team' description changed from 'The team associated with the notification subscription.' to 'The team that this notification subscription is scoped to. Null if the subscription targets a different entity type.' (UserNotificationSubscription.team)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType' description changed from 'The type of user view to which the notification subscription context is associated with.' to 'The type of user-specific view that further scopes a user notification subscription. Null if the subscription is not associated with a user view type.' (UserNotificationSubscription.userContextViewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'A user notification subscription.' on type 'UserNotificationSubscription' has changed to 'A notification subscription scoped to a specific user view. The subscriber receives notifications for events in the context of a particular user's activity view.' (UserNotificationSubscription)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'UserPayload' has description 'User operation response.' (UserPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The different permission roles available to users on an organization.' on type 'UserRoleType' has changed to 'The different permission roles available to users in a workspace.' (UserRoleType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.calendarHash' description changed from 'Hash for the user to be used in calendar URLs.' to 'A unique hash for the user, used to construct secure calendar subscription URLs.' (UserSettings.calendarHash)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.feedSummarySchedule' description changed from 'The user's feed summary schedule preference.' to 'The user's preferred schedule for receiving feed summary digests.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.notificationCategoryPreferences' description changed from 'The user's notification category preferences.' to 'The user's notification category preferences, indicating which notification categories are enabled or disabled per notification channel.' (UserSettings.notificationCategoryPreferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.notificationChannelPreferences' description changed from 'The user's notification channel preferences.' to 'The user's notification channel preferences, indicating which notification delivery channels (email, in-app, mobile push, Slack) are enabled.' (UserSettings.notificationChannelPreferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.subscribedToChangelog' description changed from 'Whether this user is subscribed to changelog email or not.' to 'Whether this user is subscribed to receive changelog emails about Linear product updates.' (UserSettings.subscribedToChangelog)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.subscribedToDPA' description changed from 'Whether this user is subscribed to DPA emails or not.' to 'Whether this user is subscribed to receive Data Processing Agreement (DPA) related emails.' (UserSettings.subscribedToDPA)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'UserSettings.subscribedToInviteAccepted' description changed from 'Whether this user is subscribed to invite accepted emails or not.' to 'Whether this user is subscribed to receive email notifications when their workspace invitations are accepted.'   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The settings of a user as a JSON object.' on type 'UserSettings' has changed to 'Per-user settings and preferences for a workspace member.   feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'UserSettingsTheme' has description 'The user's resolved theme configuration for a specific color mode and device type.' (UserSettingsTheme)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'UserUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating the authenticated user.' (UserUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ViewPreferences.preferences' description changed from 'The view preferences' to 'The view preferences values, containing layout, grouping, sorting, and field visibility settings.' (ViewPreferences.preferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ViewPreferences.type' description changed from 'The view preference type.' to 'The type of view preferences: "organization" for workspace-wide defaults or "user" for personal overrides.' (ViewPreferences.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'ViewPreferences.viewType' description changed from 'The view type.' to 'The type of view these preferences apply to, such as board, cycle, project, customView, myIssues, etc.' (ViewPreferences.viewType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'View preferences.' on type 'ViewPreferences' has changed to 'The display preferences for a view, controlling layout mode (list, board, spreadsheet), grouping, sorting, column visibility, and other visual settings. View preferences exist at two levels: organization-wide defaults and per-user overrides. The effective preferences are computed by merging both layers, with user preferences taking priority.' (ViewPreferences)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Input field 'ViewPreferencesCreateInput.type' description changed from 'The type of view preferences (either user or organization level preferences).' to 'The type of view preferences (either user or workspace level preferences).' (ViewPreferencesCreateInput.type)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ViewPreferencesPayload' has description 'The result of a view preferences mutation.' (ViewPreferencesPayload)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Description 'The type of view preferences (either user or organization level preferences).' on type 'ViewPreferencesType' has changed to 'The type of view preferences (either user or workspace level preferences).' (ViewPreferencesType)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Object type 'ViewPreferencesUpdateInput' has description 'Input for updating view preferences.' (ViewPreferencesUpdateInput)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'columnOrderBoard' was added to object type 'ViewPreferencesValues' (ViewPreferencesValues.columnOrderBoard)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'columnOrderList' was added to object type 'ViewPreferencesValues' (ViewPreferencesValues.columnOrderList)

  feat(schema): [non_breaking] Field 'continuousPipelineReleaseFieldReleaseDate' was added to object type 'ViewPreferencesValues' (ViewPreferencesValues.continuousPipelineReleaseFieldReleaseDate)
